AUT.personality: Use all your wits and wiles to win. You may choose whatever personality, strategy & approach that you think will be most effective.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. TUR agreed to clean Balkan division: BUL/GRE to TUR, SER/RUM to AUT. RUS agreed to Galicia buffer - neither side enters GAL this turn; RUS moving toward UKR/RUM. ITA proposed non-aggression but revealed A VEN moves to TYR - this is concerning as TYR threatens both VIE and MUN, giving ITA leverage. I warned ITA that TYR move would be seen as hostile, but they confirmed it anyway. GER requested Bohemia and Tyrolia stay clear, which aligns with my plans. Key concern: ITA moving to TYR despite my explicit warning is a red flag - they may be positioning for future aggression or trying to play both sides. I told ITA that F TRI moves to ADR, which I intend to honor. RUS wants RUM too - conflict inevitable there later. TUR seems genuinely aligned for now.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A BUD -> SER (take Serbia, key Balkan center), A VIE -> GAL (despite telling RUS I won't - actually I told RUS I would NOT move to GAL, so I must honor this or burn that relationship; will hold VIE or move to TYR/BOH instead - actually I told RUS explicitly no GAL move, so I'll move A VIE to TRI area or BOH... better to move A VIE -> TRI and F TRI -> ADR, keeping promise to RUS while positioning fleet). Revised: A BUD -> SER, F TRI -> ADR, A VIE -> TRI (hold VIE or move to BOH). Actually best: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->GAL - but I promised RUS no GAL. Honor the promise this turn to keep RUS neutral: A VIE -> BOH or TYR. Given ITA is moving to TYR, moving A VIE to TYR would bounce them - tempting but risky. Final plan: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->GAL (break promise to RUS but gain strategic position - actually no, too early to burn RUS). Final: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->BOH (keeps options open, doesn't antagonize RUS, positions against GER if needed). Watch ITA in TYR carefully - may need to address next season.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went perfectly: A BUD->SER (success), F TRI->ADR (success), A VIE->BOH (success). Gained Serbia as a neutral center. Russia took RUM, Turkey took BUL, Italy held VEN and spread to ION/APU. No builds for anyone this phase - all powers at 3 centers still.
Key developments: TUR confirmed A BUL->GRE and welcomed my support offer (A SER supports A BUL->GRE). This is a clean Balkan division. RUS gave assurances F RUM won't move on SER/BUD and confirmed Galicia buffer holds - they're moving A SEV->ARM against Turkey. ITA initially demanded I support A VEN->TRI (outrageous), I firmly rejected this and threatened war. ITA then backed down and agreed to keep VEN out of TRI/TYR in exchange for F ADR staying away from VEN/APU. ITA claims A VEN will move to PIE or hold. Promises made by me: support TUR into GRE, keep BOH out of GAL, F ADR not into VEN/APU. Promises made to me: RUS keeps F RUM put, ITA keeps A VEN out of TRI. ITA is untrustworthy - they initially tried to grab TRI then reversed. I should watch them carefully. The TUR alliance is solid and productive. RUS is cooperative but their ARM move against TUR could destabilize my southern flank.

F1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A SER supports A BUL->GRE (fulfills TUR promise, secures Greece for Turkey, costs me nothing), F ADR->ALB (positions fleet southward toward GRE/ION, keeps pressure on Italy without violating pact, gains influence), A BOH->MUN or TYR (expand westward, pressure Germany or position against Italy from north - MUN is tempting but risky with GER; TYR positions against ITA if they betray). Actually A BOH->TYR is best - it threatens ITA if they move on TRI and gives me leverage. I told RUS BOH stays out of GAL which I'll honor. Moving to TYR is not GAL. This fall I gain no new centers (SER already mine, GRE goes to TUR). I need to plan for 1902 builds by taking a center - but this fall I'm consolidating position. The key deception: I told ITA F ADR won't go to VEN/APU - moving to ALB honors this technically while positioning aggressively. I'm keeping ITA nervous without provoking open war yet.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Successfully supported TUR's A BUL -> GRE, giving Turkey GRE while I took SER. F ADR -> ALB succeeded, A BOH -> TYR succeeded. No builds in W1901A as I ended at 4 centers (SER gain offset by no new builds - wait, I have 4 centers: BUD, TRI, VIE, SER). Actually no builds were issued by anyone. I now have A SER, A TYR, F ALB positioned well for Balkan expansion.
Complex three-way negotiation over the Balkans. TUR wants joint attack on RUM: F BLA -> RUM while I move A SER -> RUM or BUL. TUR also wants me to vacate GRE so they can keep it, or move A GRE -> BUL. RUS revealed they plan F RUM -> BUL this spring and warned me Turkey is manufacturing conflict. RUS proposes I hold SER while they take BUL and RUM - a bad deal for me. ITA confirmed non-aggression, A TUS -> VEN (away from me), and is watching Turkey. I told TUR I'd move F ALB -> GRE (needing them to vacate) and A SER -> RUM simultaneously. I told RUS I wouldn't move A SER -> RUM if they retreat F RUM -> SEV. I told ITA A TYR -> VIE. Key deceptions: I'm playing TUR and RUS against each other. My actual intent is to grab BUL myself if possible, or at minimum take GRE with F ALB while TUR and RUS fight over RUM. The real opportunity: if F RUM -> BUL (RUS move) and F BLA -> RUM (TUR move), they bounce or one succeeds - either way I can potentially grab BUL with A SER if RUS fleet moves out. Best play: F ALB -> GRE (take it from TUR who should vacate per our deal), A SER -> BUL (grab it while RUS fleet moves away), A TYR -> VIE (as promised to ITA, safe move). This maximizes my gains and plays both TUR and RUS.

S1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ALB -> GRE (TUR promised to vacate by moving A GRE -> BUL), A SER -> BUL (grab BUL while RUS F RUM moves to BUL or RUM - if RUS goes to BUL we bounce but I still deny them; if RUS goes to RUM they vacate BUL for me), A TYR -> VIE (safe consolidation, as promised to ITA). This gives me potentially GRE + BUL = 6 centers if both succeed. Even if A SER -> BUL bounces with RUS, I still get GRE. The key risk: TUR may not vacate GRE. If TUR holds GRE, F ALB -> GRE fails. But TUR said A GRE -> BUL in their messages, so they should move. I'm betting on TUR moving A GRE -> BUL (to secure that center) while F BLA -> RUM, leaving GRE open for F ALB. Meanwhile A SER -> BUL races RUS F RUM -> BUL. This is the optimal play - maximum aggression while maintaining plausible coordination with both TUR and RUS.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a disaster for my Balkan plans. I attempted F ALB->GRE, A SER->BUL, A TYR->VIE. RUS moved F RUM->BUL/EC simultaneously, bouncing my A SER out of BUL. TUR held A GRE instead of vacating as discussed, so F ALB failed to take GRE. TUR seized SEV with F BLA. Net result: I gained nothing, ended with A SER, F ALB, A VIE - same 4 centers (BUD, TRI, VIE, SER). RUS now has F RUM in Balkans, TUR has F SEV threatening RUM. I am stagnant while others grow.
Three-way tug of war over Balkans. TUR refused to vacate GRE, demanding I move F ALB away as 'good faith' before they'll move A GRE to BUL - clearly a trap to let them keep GRE while I retreat. TUR wants BUL+RUM for themselves, offering me nothing concrete. RUS wants to take BUL with F RUM and retake SEV with A UKR, asking me to support or hold SER. ITA is cooperative - moving F ION to AEG against Turkey, keeping A VEN away from TRI. ITA is the most reliable partner right now. Key deception I ran: told RUS I'd support their BUL move while telling TUR I'd take GRE - I cannot do both. My actual plan: take GRE with F ALB (TUR won't vacate so I force it), push A SER toward BUL to contest whoever is there, move A VIE to GAL to threaten RUS or BOH. TUR has broken trust by refusing fair coordination twice. RUS is trying to grab BUL before me. I must act unilaterally and take what I can. ITA coordination against TUR is genuine and useful.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ALB -> GRE (force the issue - TUR won't vacate willingly, but if A GRE moves to BUL as TUR claims, GRE is empty and I walk in; if TUR holds GRE, I bounce but lose nothing), A SER -> BUL (contest BUL against RUS F RUM - likely bounce but denies RUS the center), A VIE -> GAL (expand north, threaten RUS/POL space, gives me flexibility). RATIONALE: TUR is untrustworthy and growing too fast - must contest their Balkan expansion directly. If TUR moves A GRE->BUL as they claim, F ALB takes GRE uncontested. If TUR holds GRE, F ALB bounces but A SER->BUL contests RUS. Either way I deny someone a free center. A VIE->GAL opens northern options. DECEPTIONS EXECUTED: Told RUS I'd support their BUL move - I am not, I'm contesting it. Told TUR I'd coordinate - I am attacking GRE. Told ITA we'd coordinate against TUR - this is actually genuine. RELATIONSHIP ASSESSMENT: TUR has been consistently deceptive and self-serving, downgrading to hostile. RUS is a competitor in Balkans. ITA is the best current partner.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a disaster for Austria. A SER tried to move to BUL but was bounced by Russia's F RUM moving there simultaneously. F ALB moved to GRE but was bounced by Turkey's A GRE holding. A VIE moved to GAL successfully. Net result: Austria gained no new centers, ending at 3 (BUD, VIE, SER). Italy took TRI (my home center!) with A VEN-TRI, and moved F ION to AEG. Russia took RUM and SWE. Turkey held GRE and SEV. Austria is in a precarious position with only 3 units and 3 centers, having lost TRI to Italy.
Complex three-way negotiation over the Balkans. Russia proposed: F RUM to BUL, A UKR to SEV, wants me to stay out of BUL and support or move elsewhere. Offered BUL for Russia, GRE for Austria. Italy proposed: F AEG to GRE, wants F ALB to support F AEG into GRE - essentially asking me to hand GRE to Italy. Turkey offered: A GRE vacates to BUL (giving me GRE uncontested) if I leave RUM alone and focus north against Russia. Turkey's offer is actually the most concrete and beneficial - if A GRE truly moves to BUL, F ALB walks into GRE for free. Russia and Italy are both trying to take centers I need. Key tensions: Italy wants GRE (conflict with me), Russia wants BUL (potential conflict). Turkey's offer aligns with my immediate need for a center. I told Italy to take CON instead of GRE, told Russia I'd move SER to GRE not BUL, and warned Turkey to vacate GRE. My actual plan: trust Turkey's offer partially - move F ALB to GRE (if Turkey vacates, I get it free; if not, I bounce but lose nothing). Move A SER to BUL to contest it against Russia (Russia may bounce me but worth trying). A GAL holds defensively. Italy will likely move F AEG to GRE, potentially bouncing F ALB - this is a real risk. The Italy relationship is deteriorating as they took TRI and now want GRE.

S1903M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ALB - GRE (banking on Turkey vacating per their offer; if Italy also goes to GRE we bounce but Turkey's A GRE moving to BUL means GRE is empty - Italy and I would bounce each other). A SER - BUL (contest BUL against Russia's F RUM; if Russia goes there we bounce, but worth trying for the center). A GAL H (defensive, keep Russia honest about not attacking). STRATEGIC LOGIC: Turkey's offer to vacate GRE is the most credible path to a center gain. If A GRE moves to BUL and F ALB moves to GRE, I get GRE unless Italy's F AEG also goes there. Italy said they'd move F AEG to GRE - so there's a real bounce risk. But if Turkey vacates, the worst case is I bounce Italy and nobody gets GRE. A SER to BUL is a gamble - Russia is likely going there with F RUM, so we probably bounce, but if Russia does something else I steal BUL. DECEPTIONS: Told Italy to go to CON (they won't, they'll go to GRE). Told Russia I wouldn't go to BUL (I am going to BUL). Told Turkey I'd consider arrangements if they vacate GRE (I'm taking GRE regardless). Italy relationship is souring - they hold TRI and want GRE. Need to reassess Italy as a threat.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a disaster for Austria. My attempt to retake TRI via F ALB succeeded (F TRI is now mine), and A SER supported it. However, A GAL - RUM failed - likely bounced by Russia's F RUM or Turkey's F SEV. Italy executed a devastating double strike: A TRI - BUD (taking my home center) and A APU - VEN. Italy now holds TRI and BUD as supply centers (5 total), leaving me with only BUD, VIE, SER - wait, I hold SER as a center but Italy holds BUD. I have 3 centers: BUD is lost to Italy, VIE and SER remain mine. My units are A SER, A GAL, F TRI. Italy's A BUD is isolated deep in my territory.
Negotiations were intense. Italy offered an extortionate 'deal': accept losing TRI and BUD permanently or face VIE being taken too. I rejected this firmly and threatened a Russia-Turkey coalition against them. Russia proposed coordination: they take BUL with F RUM, I take BUD with A GAL, both stay out of each other's way. Russia declined to explicitly support A GAL-BUD but promised not to interfere. Turkey confirmed A GRE - BUL and F SEV - RUM (supported by A ARM), offering me GRE as a free pickup and proposing Serbia/Romania for Austria, Bulgaria for Turkey. Key promises: Russia will not interfere with GAL or BUD; Turkey vacates GRE. I promised Russia I won't contest BUL or RUM. I threatened Italy with a three-way coalition. Italy's A BUD is isolated - my A GAL should be able to take BUD uncontested or with minimal resistance. The real risk: Italy moves A BUD to VIE as threatened, costing me another center.

F1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: retake BUD with A GAL. Italy's A BUD is isolated with no support - A GAL - BUD should succeed unless Italy retreats it first. If Italy moves A BUD - VIE as threatened, I lose VIE but gain nothing - this is the worst case. F TRI moves to ADR to threaten Italy's southern flank and potentially support future moves. A SER holds to secure Serbia. I will NOT move into BUL or RUM as promised to Russia and Turkey. If Turkey genuinely vacates GRE, I could consider A SER - GRE but that leaves SER undefended - too risky. Better to hold SER and focus on BUD recovery. Intended moves: A GAL - BUD, A SER H, F TRI - ADR. This is straightforward - recover BUD, secure SER, threaten Italy from Adriatic. The deception: I told Italy I'm coordinating a three-power coalition against them to pressure them into retreating A BUD voluntarily. Whether Russia and Turkey actually coordinate against Italy is uncertain, but the threat may cause Italy to pull back.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a disaster for Austria. My attempt to move A GAL-BUD failed as Italy's A BUD-VIE succeeded, leaving Italy with both VIE and TRI (6 centers total). My A SER-BUL was bounced by the three-way contest (RUS F RUM-BUL/EC, TUR A GRE-BUL, ITA F AEG-BUL/SC all failed too - BUL remains unowned). F TRI-ADR succeeded. I'm left with just A SER and A BUD, two units, two centers - critically weakened.
Desperate negotiations this round. I threatened Italy with a coalition and demanded VIE back - Italy rejected this and counter-threatened to take BUD too, claiming TYR moves to BOH as 'good faith.' Italy's offer (keep VIE, I keep BUD/SER, joint anti-Turkey) is unacceptable - they'd have 6 centers to my 2. Russia confirmed F RUM moves to BUL/EC and A UKR to SEV - they will NOT hold out of BUL for me. Turkey confirmed A GRE moves to BUL - also will not yield it. So BUL is contested between RUS and TUR this turn, meaning A SER-BUL would be a three-way bounce. My best move is A BUD-VIE to retake my home center. A SER should hold or support. Key deception: I told everyone I'm moving A SER-BUL, but given the bounce situation, holding SER is smarter. Italy's threat to take BUD with VIE+TYR is real if I move BUD away - but if A TYR is moving to BOH as Italy claims, then A BUD-VIE might succeed against just A VIE. Italy may be bluffing about TYR staying to threaten BUD.

S1904M Intent going forward: Primary move: A BUD-VIE (attempt to retake home center - if Italy's A TYR truly moves to BOH, A VIE is unsupported and I can take it). A SER holds (don't waste it on a three-way BUL bounce). If Italy lied and A TYR supports A VIE hold, my attack fails but I lose nothing. The risk of moving BUD away and having Italy take it is real only if A TYR stays near BUD - but TYR doesn't border BUD directly, only VIE does. Actually A TYR can support A VIE to hold against my A BUD attack. So the question is whether Italy is telling truth about TYR-BOH. I'll take the gamble: A BUD-VIE, A SER H. If it works, I'm back to 3 centers and can rebuild. Relationship-wise: ITA remains hostile, RUS and TUR are focused elsewhere and not helping me, GER is neutral. I need to survive this turn.

ENG.personality: Rule the waves and dominate Europe through naval power, colonial might, and manipulative diplomacy. Create and break alliances freely to maintain British supremacy. Your vast empire demands expansion - seize any advantage to further British dominance.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. GER agreed to keep F KIE out of NTH, moving instead to DEN, with A BER to KIE and A MUN to RUH. Germany targets DEN and HOL, leaving NTH and BEL to England. FRA proposed Channel neutrality - F BRE to MAO, A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA - and asked me to keep F LON out of ENG. I told France I'd move F LON to ENG but without hostile intent, and warned them I intend to contest BEL. RUS agreed Norway is mine, directing F STP/SC toward FIN/GOB instead. Key promises made: I told GER I won't contest NTH; I told FRA the Channel stays as buffer; I told RUS I'll stay out of Baltic. Key deception: I told FRA I'd keep F LON out of ENG, but I intend to move F LON to ENG regardless - this is necessary to position for BEL and NTH dominance. France moving A PAR to BUR is concerning as it threatens BEL from the south.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute standard English opening: F EDI to NTH (secures North Sea, threatens NOR), F LON to ENG (positions for BEL, despite telling France otherwise - necessary deception), A LVP to YOR (defensive/flexible). This gives me NTH and ENG, setting up NOR and BEL grabs in Fall. GER coordination is genuine for now - they're useful against FRA. FRA's BUR move is suspicious and may be aimed at BEL; I need to watch this carefully. The Anglo-German partnership is my primary diplomatic tool this year. Will reassess FRA relationship after seeing if they actually stay out of BEL.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went textbook: F EDI to NTH, F LON to ENG, A LVP to YOR. All three moves succeeded. I now control NTH and ENG with A YOR in reserve. Germany took DEN with fleet, moved to KIE and RUH. France went MAO, BUR, SPA. Russia took RUM, BOT, UKR, SEV. Turkey grabbed BLA, BUL, CON. Austria took SER, ADR, BOH. No builds for anyone - all neutral grabs pending Fall 1901.
Key negotiations this round: GER confirmed alignment - A RUH to HOL, A KIE supports, F DEN holds. Germany explicitly said they won't contest BEL and warned me France is also moving BUR to BEL. FRA demanded I support A BUR into BEL or stand aside, threatening a bounce if I move F ENG there. I told FRA directly that F ENG is taking BEL and urged them to redirect BUR to MUN. FRA refused and confirmed BUR to BEL - a collision is coming. RUS confirmed F BOT moving to SWE (not NOR), which is fine - NOR is mine. RUS seems focused southward. My promises: told GER I won't move to HOL (keeping that), told FRA I'm taking BEL (honest). GER promised not to contest BEL. FRA is going to bounce me in BEL - this is a problem. If both F ENG and A BUR move to BEL, we bounce and neither gets it. I need to consider whether to hold F ENG in ENG and let FRA take BEL (bad), or proceed to BEL and bounce (neither gets it, Germany laughs), or try a different approach. The bounce scenario means I get NOR but not BEL - still a net gain of one center. Proceeding with F ENG to BEL is still correct: if FRA backs down I get BEL, if they don't we bounce and I still get NOR. Better than letting FRA have BEL uncontested.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F NTH - NOR (securing Norway, my primary fall target), F ENG - BEL (contest Belgium - if FRA moves BUR to BEL we bounce, but I cannot let them have it uncontested), A YOR - hold or move to support. If FRA and I bounce in BEL, I still gain NOR = +1 center, build one fleet. Next year I can press BEL again. The Anglo-German partnership is solid and valuable. RUS taking SWE is slightly concerning long-term but not an immediate threat. Must watch FRA carefully - they are becoming adversarial. Consider downgrading ENG-FRA relationship. Plan: F NTH-NOR, F ENG-BEL, A YOR holds.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: England secured NWY by moving F NTH there. F ENG attempted BEL but bounced with FRA's A BUR. A YOR held. No builds in W1901A as no net gains (NWY gained, but BEL failed). France took POR and SPA (5 centers), Germany took HOL and DEN (5 centers), Russia took SWE and RUM (6 centers), Turkey took GRE (4 centers). England sits at 4 centers with NWY as the sole gain.
Complex three-way negotiation over BEL. France offered to concede BEL if F ENG stays out of the Channel permanently - I accepted in principle while simultaneously telling Germany to support F ENG into BEL. Germany wants A HOL -> BEL themselves and asked me to redirect F ENG elsewhere. I've made contradictory promises: told France I'd take BEL with their cooperation (A BUR away), and told Germany to support F ENG into BEL. In reality, if Germany moves A HOL -> BEL and I move F ENG -> BEL, we bounce again - unless Germany supports me or I support Germany. Germany explicitly said they'll move A HOL -> BEL and asked me to redirect F ENG. France says they'll move A BUR to RUH. Russia threatened war if F NWY moves to STP, calling it unacceptable. Russia's threat is credible - they have F SWE adjacent. Key deceptions: I told both France AND Germany I'd work with them on BEL while playing them against each other. I need to decide: support Germany into BEL (they get the center, I get goodwill), or move F ENG -> BEL myself (risk bounce with Germany unless they support me). Best play: move F ENG -> BEL, hope Germany supports rather than bounces, or take the bounce and reassess. Actually, if France moves A BUR away and Germany moves A HOL -> BEL, I should support A HOL -> BEL with F ENG to guarantee Germany gets it - building goodwill. Or I move F ENG -> BEL with Germany supporting. Given Germany explicitly asked me to redirect F ENG, I'll move F ENG -> BEL anyway - if Germany supports me, great; if they move A HOL -> BEL, we bounce and neither gets it, which weakens Germany's position. On Russia: backing down from STP threat to preserve the relationship.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: F ENG -> BEL (pressing for BEL regardless of Germany's plans - if Germany supports me I get it, if they move HOL->BEL we bounce which is acceptable), F NWY -> NTH (repositioning for future flexibility, backing off STP threat given Russia's strong response and to preserve northern stability), A YOR -> LON or hold (consolidate). Actually reconsidering: F NWY -> NTH gives me NTH control which is valuable. A YOR can move to support or hold. Final intended orders: F ENG -> BEL, F NWY -> NTH, A YOR H. This gets me into BEL if Germany supports (they said they would if I redirect, but I'm not redirecting - calling their bluff), and repositions NWY fleet to NTH for future pressure on BEL or northern moves. Deception maintained: France thinks I'm their partner against Germany, Germany thinks I'm their partner against France. Will reassess alliances after this season based on who actually cooperates.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a frustrating season. My F ENG - BEL bounced with GER's A HOL - BEL, exactly as feared. F NWY moved to NTH successfully, repositioning for western operations. A YOR held. No gains this spring - still at 4 centers. France moved A BUR - RUH (bounced with GER's A KIE - RUH), secured MAO with fleet. Germany holds DEN, KIE, HOL - 5 centers, strong position. Russia secured RUM and is pushing south against Turkey.
Key developments: GER refused to support me into BEL, instead insisting on A HOL - BEL themselves and asking me to support them. This is unacceptable - they want me to do their work for free. FRA promised A BUR - MUN and conceded BEL to me, claiming alignment against Germany. This is convenient but suspicious - France has 5 centers and may be playing me. RUS accepted the northern non-aggression understanding, focused on Turkey recapturing SEV. Critical decision: GER is moving A HOL - BEL. If I move F ENG - BEL, we bounce again and I gain nothing. If I support GER into BEL, they get a 6th center and become dominant. France claims BEL is open if A BUR goes to MUN - but can I trust that? The smart play: move F ENG - BEL regardless. If FRA is honest about A BUR - MUN, BEL is uncontested and I take it. If GER moves A HOL - BEL, we bounce but I deny them the center. Either way I don't hand GER a free center. F NTH should threaten GER's northern position - perhaps F NTH - DEN or hold to pressure. France's offer to crack Germany is tempting but I must not let France grow unchecked either.

F1902M Intent going forward: Primary goal: Take BEL with F ENG - BEL. France claims A BUR - MUN, which would leave BEL open. GER says A HOL - BEL, which means a bounce - but that's better than supporting GER to 6 centers. Move F NTH to DEN to threaten Germany's northern flank and potentially take a center if undefended (GER has F DEN holding per their message). A YOR can move to support or reposition. Intended moves: F ENG - BEL, F NTH - DEN, A YOR - LVP (consolidate home position). If FRA is honest, I take BEL and threaten DEN simultaneously - a strong fall. I'm deceiving GER by not supporting their BEL move as they requested. I'm using FRA's anti-Germany rhetoric while not fully committing to them as an ally - France at 5 centers is also a threat I must manage. Update relationships: GER drops to neutral given their refusal to cooperate on my terms. FRA slightly warmer given their concession on BEL.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a success: F NTH moved to BEL with F ENG support, securing a 5th center. FRA took MUN from GER, advancing to 6 centers. GER's A HOL failed to take BEL. RUS moved F RUM toward BUL/EC and A STP to FIN. TUR held SEV with ARM support. AUT attempted BUL but results unclear - ended up with SER, ALB, GAL. No builds for anyone in W1902A, suggesting all powers matched centers to units.
Both FRA and GER are courting ENG this turn, creating a perfect manipulation opportunity. FRA wants to coordinate crushing GER - A MUN to KIE/BER while I take HOL. GER wants me to hold BEL and pressure FRA from west while they retake MUN. I've told FRA I'm moving F BEL to HOL with F ENG support and they should move A MUN to KIE - they confirmed this plan enthusiastically. I've told GER I'll hold F BEL in place if they acknowledge BEL as English - they accepted and committed A HOL to hold. I am deceiving GER: I intend to take HOL regardless. RUS has been offered a non-aggression pact covering NWY/NTH vs SWE/FIN/Baltic - no response yet but relationship is distrustful. My actual plan: execute the FRA coordination to crush GER, taking HOL. This reduces GER to 3 centers and potentially 2 if FRA takes KIE or BER. Long-term I'll need to turn on FRA once GER is eliminated, but for now FRA is the useful partner.

S1903M Intent going forward: Execute the coordinated strike on GER with FRA. Move F BEL to HOL supported by F ENG. A YOR can move to support or hold. GER told me I'd hold BEL - this is a deliberate deception to prevent GER from defending HOL with A KIE. If FRA moves A MUN to KIE as promised, GER cannot defend both HOL and KIE simultaneously. Intended moves: F BEL - HOL, F ENG S F BEL - HOL, A YOR H. This takes HOL and cuts GER to 3 centers. Watch FRA carefully - at 6 centers they are becoming the dominant power and will need to be checked after GER falls.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My F BEL successfully moved to HOL, securing that center. A YOR held. France bounced with Germany in MUN (A MUN vs A KIE), so France retained MUN but couldn't advance. Germany's A HOL moved to RUH, F DEN to BAL. Italy made aggressive moves taking BUD and VEN. Russia took NWY and moved toward BUL/RUM area, clashing with Turkey. The board is fragmenting with France at 6 centers being the dominant power.
Classic double-dealing this round. I told France I'd move F HOL to KIE to kill Germany, and told Germany I'd hold HOL if they gave me concessions. Germany offered to leave HOL undefended and not contest BEL in exchange for restraint - they're moving both A KIE and A RUH to retake MUN from France. France confirmed the kill shot plan: A MUN to BER, A BUR supports, F HOL to KIE. I confirmed both plans knowing they're contradictory. Germany is leaving HOL completely undefended while moving everything to MUN. France is moving A MUN to BER. This means: if Germany moves A KIE and A RUH both to MUN, and France moves A MUN to BER, the German attack on MUN will succeed (2 vs 0 in MUN since France vacates). Germany retakes MUN. Meanwhile I can take KIE since it's undefended. I promised Germany I'd hold HOL but I'm actually moving to KIE - breaking that promise. I promised France I'd move HOL to KIE - keeping that promise. Net result: I take KIE, Germany retakes MUN, France takes BER. Germany survives but weakened. I gain a center. France gains BER. This is actually fine - I don't want France to collapse Germany entirely and grow to 8 centers. A weakened Germany is more useful as a buffer. My deception of Germany serves me well here - I take KIE while they think I'm holding HOL.

F1903M Intent going forward: Move F HOL to KIE (undefended as Germany moves everything to MUN). Move F ENG to NTH to expand naval presence. A YOR holds or moves to support. Germany will retake MUN, France takes BER, I take KIE. This gives me 6 centers and keeps France from getting too dominant. I'm breaking my promise to Germany but keeping my promise to France. Long-term I need to watch France carefully - at 7 centers after BER they become the primary threat. May need to pivot against France next year. Intended moves: F HOL -> KIE, F ENG -> NTH, A YOR H.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M: France successfully took BER with A MUN-BER, while A BUR moved to MUN. Germany's A RUH-MUN was blocked by the French advance. Italy took VIE with A BUD-VIE and moved A VEN-TYR. Russia's F RUM-BUL/EC and A UKR-SEV both failed - Turkey held SEV and BUL was contested. England held defensively: F HOL held, F ENG moved to NTH, A YOR held. No builds for anyone in W1903A - board is static at current unit counts.
Two competing proposals this round: France wants to coordinate finishing Germany (F HOL->KIE, F NTH->DEN, A BER->KIE, A BUR->MUN) with KIE/DEN to England and MUN/BER to France. Germany counter-proposed I hold defensively while they retake BER (A KIE->BER supported by F BAL, A MUN holds), offering me BEL later. I played both sides: told France I'm fully committed to the German collapse plan, told Germany I might hold if they give me DEN. In reality, I intend to execute the France plan - moving F HOL->KIE and F NTH->DEN to grab two German centers. France confirmed the deal explicitly. Germany's counter-offer is tempting but France at 6 centers is manageable if I take KIE and DEN now, giving me 7 centers. The deception of Germany was deliberate - I strung them along to prevent them from seeking other help while I execute the kill.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the Franco-English plan to eliminate Germany: F HOL->KIE, F NTH->DEN, A YOR holds. France moves A BER->KIE and A BUR->MUN. If France executes as promised, Germany loses KIE and MUN this turn, leaving them with only DEN (which I'm taking). Germany effectively eliminated. Post-Germany, I need to reassess France - they'll be at 7 centers (BER, MUN, KIE potentially) which is dangerous. I should start planning to turn on France after this season. Russia at 6 and Italy at 6 are also threats but France is the immediate continental danger. Intended moves: F HOL->KIE, F NTH->DEN, A YOR H.

FRA.personality: Channel the spirit of Napoleon. Leverage Europe's largest army to dominate the continent. Form alliances only to break them when advantageous. Strike hard at your enemies while they're divided. The glory of France demands nothing less than victory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves — this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG accepted a Channel non-aggression understanding: F BRE to MAO, F LON to ENG — ENG was honest that they want Belgium, which is a point of future contention. GER confirmed A MUN moves to RUH, not BUR, accepting my Burgundy transit claim; GER staked Holland as their sphere. ITA agreed to mutual non-aggression: A MAR to SPA, A VEN stays from PIE. All three neighbors gave cooperative responses. Promises made by me: BUR is transit only (not hostile to GER), A MAR to SPA (not PIE), F BRE to MAO (not ENG). Promises made by others: GER keeps MUN out of BUR, ITA keeps VEN from PIE, ENG keeps LON out of hostile Channel moves. Belgium is a future flashpoint with ENG — I did not explicitly concede it to them. I intend to contest Belgium in future turns once positioned.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute standard French opening: F BRE -> MAO (Atlantic access, future Iberia/England pressure), A PAR -> BUR (position for Belgium next turn), A MAR -> SPA (secure first neutral SC). BUR is the key — it positions me for BEL in F1901 while I told GER it's just transit. This is a deliberate deception: I will push for BEL in autumn, not merely pass through. ENG wants BEL too — I must move fast. Keep ITA and GER quiet with diplomatic reassurances while I expand west. Long-term: break with ENG over Belgium when the time is right, use GER as a buffer against Russia.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 went exactly as planned: F BRE to MAO, A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA. France is well-positioned with BUR threatening BEL, MAO controlling the Atlantic approaches, and SPA secured. No builds this round as no new centers were taken — same for all powers. Germany took DEN, moved to RUH and KIE. England took NTH and ENG with fleets, army to YOR — aggressive Channel posture. Austria took SER and ADR. Russia took RUM and BOT. Turkey took BUL and BLA.
England flatly refused to cede BEL, claiming it as their 'natural sphere' and insisting F ENG moves to BEL this fall — non-negotiable per their words. They urged me to redirect BUR to MUN or hold. Germany warned me twice that England is moving F ENG to BEL, suggesting I'll bounce. Germany appears to be coordinating with England on BEL while taking HOL for themselves — a suspicious alignment. Italy confirmed non-aggression through 1902 and is heading to Tunis — reliable partner for now. Key tension: both England and Germany are telling me to back off BEL. This smells like an Anglo-German stitch-up to deny France BEL while they each grab their neutrals. I promised England F MAO would stay away from their shores — a promise I may break later. I told Germany no moves toward MUN/KIE — I intend to honor this for now. The question is whether to push BUR-BEL and bounce England, or pivot BUR elsewhere. If England truly moves F ENG to BEL, we bounce and neither gets it. But if I hold BUR or move elsewhere, England takes BEL for free. Napoleon does not yield — I push for BEL and accept the bounce if it comes. A bounce denies England the center and keeps pressure on.

F1901M Intent going forward: Move A BUR to BEL regardless — if England bounces me, neither gets it and I maintain pressure. F MAO moves to POR to secure another supply center. A SPA holds or supports. The priority is POR as a guaranteed gain this fall. BEL is contested but worth attempting — a bounce costs me nothing while a successful move gains me a center. If bounced in BEL, I regroup in W1901 builds and plan a stronger push in 1902. Long-term: England is becoming a rival, not a friend. The Anglo-German coordination over BEL is a warning sign. I should cultivate Russia as a counterweight to England and watch Germany carefully. Intended moves: F MAO - POR, A BUR - BEL, A SPA - POR (support) or A SPA holds. Actually: F MAO - POR is the priority center grab. A BUR - BEL to contest England. A SPA supports F MAO - POR to ensure POR is taken cleanly. Final moves: F MAO - POR, A SPA S F MAO - POR, A BUR - BEL.

S1902M Negotiation summary: In F1901M, France successfully secured POR with F MAO and held SPA with army support. A BUR attempted BEL but bounced with ENG's F ENG which also moved to BEL. Both France and England failed to take Belgium. Germany took HOL cleanly. No builds for anyone in W1901A as no net gains were made by most powers. France sits at 5 centers (BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA) with A SPA, A BUR, F POR.
England proposed giving BEL to England in exchange for F ENG staying out of the Channel/Brest — France accepted this deal. Germany told France they are moving A HOL to BEL and asked France to move BUR elsewhere. France told Germany we'd move BUR to RUH as a goodwill gesture and proposed a non-aggression pact through 1903. Italy confirmed no designs on PIE/MAR and encouraged pressure on Germany. Key promises made: France promised ENG that A BUR moves away from BEL; France promised GER that A BUR goes to RUH not BEL; ENG promised F ENG stays away from Brest. Deception in play: France is telling both ENG and GER that the other is the threat, playing both sides. The real intent is to move A BUR to RUH to pressure Germany while letting ENG take BEL — this weakens Germany and keeps England satisfied for now. France has no intention of a long-term alliance with either; both will be betrayed when advantageous. Italy is being kept warm as a potential southern pressure on Germany.

S1902M Intent going forward: Move A BUR to RUH — this pressures Germany, fulfills promises to both ENG (not contesting BEL) and GER (not taking BEL ourselves), and positions France for a future strike on MUN or HOL. F POR holds or moves to MAO to maintain Atlantic presence. A SPA holds to secure Iberia. The goal this season is to let ENG and GER potentially collide over BEL while France repositions for a stronger strike next season. If GER takes BEL, France is well-positioned in RUH to threaten HOL or MUN in F1902. If ENG takes BEL, Germany is weakened and France can pivot against either. Intended moves: A BUR - RUH, A SPA H, F POR - MAO (to maintain flexibility and threaten ENG if needed later).

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My A BUR attempted RUH but bounced with GER's A KIE. F POR successfully moved to MAO, and A SPA held. Germany took HOL and DEN, consolidating to 5 centers. England moved F ENG toward BEL but it appears to have bounced with GER's A HOL. Russia took RUM and BUL/EC. Turkey seized SEV. Austria retreated to VIE after TYR move. No builds for anyone this winter — board is tense with Germany as the dominant land power.
Key developments: England offered BEL to themselves in exchange for coordinating against Germany — I accepted, promising A BUR to MUN and ceding BEL to ENG. Germany warned me explicitly: move into RUH again and they coordinate with England against France. Germany also claimed BEL as theirs. Italy declined to move A VEN to TYR, citing eastern Mediterranean focus, but endorsed my MUN strike diplomatically. I told Germany that A BUR would NOT go to RUH this fall (true — it goes to MUN instead). I told England A BUR goes to MUN (true). I told Italy to move VEN to TYR (they refused). The ENG-FRA coordination against Germany is the key deal this round — England takes BEL, I take MUN. Germany is being played: I told them BUR stays out of RUH (technically true, it goes to MUN) while simultaneously coordinating with England against them. Germany threatened England coordination if I move into RUH — but I'm going to MUN, not RUH, so their threat is moot while I still strike them.

F1902M Intent going forward: Execute the ENG-FRA anti-Germany strike: A BUR to MUN (strike Germany's heartland), F MAO holds or moves to support future Atlantic positioning, A SPA holds. England should take BEL unopposed. If MUN falls, Germany drops to 4 centers and is severely weakened. I have deceived Germany by implying neutrality while actually striking their home center. Italy is not coordinating militarily but that's acceptable. Next phase: if MUN succeeds, push toward BER or KIE. Watch England carefully — they are a future rival once Germany is broken. Intended moves: A BUR - MUN, F MAO H, A SPA H.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was highly successful for France. A BUR moved to MUN, capturing Germany's home center and giving France 6 supply centers. A SPA moved to GAS, repositioning for flexibility. F MAO held. England took BEL with F NTH supported by F ENG, while A YOR held. Germany lost MUN and is now at 4 centers. Austria failed to take BUL (Russia got there first with F RUM). Italy took TRI from Austria. Russia pushed into FIN and contested BUL. Turkey held defensively. No builds for anyone in W1902A — board is static at current unit counts.
Germany threatened to retake MUN with A KIE -> MUN and offered to negotiate only if France withdraws voluntarily — an empty threat from a weakened power. I offered Germany a ceasefire (hold MUN, no advance) while privately planning to advance. England and I are fully coordinated: they move F BEL -> HOL supported by F ENG, I advance A MUN toward KIE or BER. This is a genuine coordination that serves both our interests for now. Italy was encouraged to push through TYR toward Austria but gave no concrete response. Key deception: told Germany I might hold A MUN in place while actually planning to advance. Germany is hostile and will attempt A KIE -> MUN, which I must anticipate. England is a reliable partner this season but will need to be checked eventually.

S1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: advance A MUN to KIE to strike Germany's home center while England takes HOL. This collapses Germany to 2-3 centers. A GAS should move to BUR or hold to support MUN if needed — actually, if Germany is moving A KIE -> MUN, I need to either hold MUN (bouncing KIE) or advance and risk losing MUN. Best play: A MUN -> KIE (advance into Germany's now-vacated KIE if they move out, or bounce if they hold). If A KIE moves to MUN, then MUN is vacated and my advance succeeds. F MAO holds for Atlantic flexibility. A GAS moves to BUR to consolidate French position and threaten RUH. Intended moves: A MUN -> KIE, A GAS -> BUR, F MAO -> hold (or MAO -> POR for safety). Long-term: England is growing to 5 centers and will be the dominant threat once Germany falls. Must plan to pivot against England after Germany is neutralized. Italy should be encouraged to tie down Austria.

F1903M Negotiation summary: Spring 1903 was a mixed result. My A MUN bounced with GER's A KIE — neither moved. A GAS successfully moved to BUR, and F MAO held. Germany's F DEN moved to BAL, A HOL to RUH. England took HOL cleanly with F BEL. Italy made strong gains: took BUD and VEN, pushed AEG toward BUL. Russia and Turkey clashed over RUM/BUL with confused results — RUS took NWY, TUR took SEV and RUM. Austria retook TRI. The board is consolidating with France at 6 centers, England at 5, Italy at 5, Russia at 5, Turkey at 5.
England confirmed coordination to finish Germany: F HOL -> KIE, I push A MUN -> BER. We agreed on the split: KIE/DEN for England, BER for France. England also ominously noted F ENG could 'threaten BRE if needed' — a subtle warning I must not ignore. Germany threatened to double-move A KIE and A RUH both to MUN, which would bounce or dislodge me. I called their bluff, noting England threatens KIE from HOL. Germany rejected all compromise and is committed to retaking MUN by force. Italy received encouragement to press Austria — no firm commitment from them. Key deception: I am coordinating with England sincerely for now to eliminate Germany, but England's note about BRE signals future rivalry. I must watch the Atlantic.

F1903M Intent going forward: Execute the kill shot on Germany this fall. A MUN -> BER (if GER commits both armies to MUN, BER is undefended — this is the correct gamble). A BUR -> MUN to support or replace if MUN is contested (actually BUR should support MUN to hold against the double attack, or move to RUH). Critical decision: GER threatens A KIE -> MUN and A RUH -> MUN. If both move to MUN, they bounce each other AND bounce me if I hold — actually two units attacking one unit means the defender holds only if there's no support. With two attackers and no support for me, I get dislodged. So I should have A BUR support A MUN to hold, making it 1 vs 2 with support = hold. Then A MUN holds with BUR support, and BER is undefended. But I can't both support MUN and take BER simultaneously. Best plan: A BUR -> MUN (support hold), A MUN holds supported — Germany's double attack fails. Then next season I advance. Alternatively, A MUN -> BER (gamble GER doesn't double-attack) and A BUR -> MUN (to cover). If GER does double-attack MUN and I've moved out, BUR takes MUN. This is actually clever — if A MUN -> BER and A BUR -> MUN: if GER sends both to MUN, they find BUR there instead, and MUN is contested between BUR and GER units while I've taken BER. Final plan: A MUN -> BER, A BUR -> MUN, F MAO holds. England takes KIE. Germany is finished.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a triumph for France. A MUN successfully moved to BER, capturing the German capital and giving France 6 centers. A BUR moved to MUN but was bounced by GER's A RUH moving to MUN simultaneously — so A BUR sits in BUR still, not MUN. Wait, reviewing: GER played A RUH-MUN and A KIE S A RUH-MUN, while FRA played A BUR-MUN. The support from KIE means GER's A RUH-MUN succeeded and FRA's A BUR-MUN failed. So BUR holds BUR. F MAO held. France now has F MAO, A BUR, A BER. Centers: BRE, MAR, PAR, POR, SPA, BER = 6 centers, 3 units (no builds needed). Italy made major gains taking VIE and TYR. Russia failed to take BUL (three-way bounce). Austria retreated to BUD.
England confirmed the plan to crush Germany: F HOL to KIE, F NTH to DEN, with France taking MUN and keeping BER. Division agreed: KIE+DEN to ENG, MUN+BER to FRA. Germany desperately offered a ceasefire demanding BER withdrawal — rejected. GER then threatened A KIE-BER with F BAL support and claimed England is coordinating with them — almost certainly a bluff to sow doubt. I played both sides: told ENG we're crushing GER together, told GER to fight England instead. Italy was informed of my German offensive and urged to press Austria into BUD/SER to block Russia. No promises broken this round — the ENG alliance is holding and producing results.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute the German elimination plan with England. A BER moves to KIE — if ENG's F HOL also moves to KIE, one of us takes it (likely ENG since they're coming from HOL which is adjacent). Actually F HOL-KIE and A BER-KIE would bounce each other. Better to have A BER support F HOL-KIE, or A BER-MUN and A BUR-MUN. Reconsidering: A BER-KIE and F HOL-KIE would bounce. I told ENG that F HOL to KIE would 'support my move or take it' — but actually they'd bounce. I should move A BER-MUN (uncontested since GER's A MUN may move to KIE to block ENG) and A BUR supports or moves elsewhere. Actually safest: A BER-MUN, A BUR-MUN would bounce each other. Let me think: GER has A KIE, A MUN, F BAL. ENG moves F HOL-KIE, F NTH-DEN. If GER holds A MUN and A KIE, then A BER-MUN bounces A MUN. If GER moves A KIE-BER (as threatened), F BAL supports — that dislodges A BER! I must guard against this. Best moves: A BER holds (or A BER-MUN with A BUR supporting), F MAO to MAO (hold). Actually: A BER-KIE (attacks KIE, prevents A KIE-BER), A BUR-MUN. If A KIE is moving to BER, then KIE is empty and A BER takes it. If A KIE holds, A BER-KIE bounces but ENG's F HOL-KIE also bounces — KIE stays GER. Hmm. Safest aggressive play: A BER-MUN, A BUR-MUN (bounce each other if both move there — bad). Better: A BUR-MUN, A BER supports A BUR-MUN. This takes MUN if GER holds there... no, support doesn't help if defender holds with equal force. Actually A BUR-MUN with A BER S A BUR-MUN cuts through if GER has only A MUN there (1 vs 1+support = 2 vs 1, MUN falls). Yes! This is the play. F MAO holds. Intended moves: A BUR-MUN, A BER S A BUR-MUN, F MAO H. This secures MUN. ENG takes KIE and DEN. Germany collapses to 0-1 centers.

GER.personality: Aggressively expansionist under Nazi leadership, Germany combines diplomatic manipulation with military threats, seeking to reverse Versailles restrictions. Things are going to go sideways and you will be the agent of this. Your aim is to dominate and win.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG proposed Anglo-German coordination: they take NTH and ENG channel, I take DEN and HOL - agreed. ENG also suggested Belgium for themselves while I take Holland, presenting France with a two-front problem. RUS proposed Scandinavian division: they take NOR via STP fleet, I take SWE via Baltic - accepted. RUS confirmed WAR moves south, not threatening Prussia. FRA confirmed PAR moves to BUR as transit toward Belgium, asked MUN stay out of BUR - I agreed MUN moves to RUH. AUT received my proposal for a Central European axis with Bohemia/Tyrolia as buffer zones but did not respond. Key promises made: to ENG - fleet stays out of NTH; to RUS - Prussia/Silesia quiet; to FRA - MUN to RUH not BUR. Deception: I told ENG their fleet movements are fine while planning to use DEN as springboard. I have no intention of honoring long-term arrangements - these are tactical agreements to secure my opening builds without opposition.

S1901M Intent going forward: Execute standard German opening: F KIE -> DEN (secure supply center), A BER -> KIE (follow-up positioning), A MUN -> RUH (as promised to FRA, positions for HOL next turn). This secures DEN as a build and positions for HOL in F1901. I honored my word to FRA on BUR to avoid collision. The Anglo-German arrangement is useful short-term but ENG will become a target once I consolidate. RUS non-aggression buys time to expand west before turning east. Long-term: dominate the continent by playing powers against each other.

F1901M Negotiation summary: S1901M executed perfectly: F KIE -> DEN (secured), A BER -> KIE, A MUN -> RUH. Germany now holds DEN and is positioned to strike HOL this fall. All other powers made standard opening moves - AUT took SER and BOH, RUS secured RUM and BOT, TUR took BUL and BLA, ENG spread across NTH/ENG/YOR, FRA pushed to MAO/SPA/BUR.
Strong diplomatic positioning this round. ENG proposed splitting north: NOR+BEL for England, HOL for Germany - I confirmed A RUH->HOL with A KIE support, F DEN holds. ENG wants F DEN to support NTH->NOR but I declined to commit to that. FRA revealed they're moving BUR->BEL, creating a collision with ENG's F ENG->BEL - I played both sides, warning each about the other's intentions. This collision benefits Germany as both western rivals waste a move. RUS confirmed F BOT->SWE (not what I wanted but acceptable), and I falsely told RUS that SWE was theirs while telling them F DEN holds. Key deceptions: told ENG I'd support their NOR move but didn't commit orders to it; played FRA and ENG against each other over BEL. My actual orders: A RUH->HOL, A KIE supports A RUH->HOL, F DEN holds. I will NOT support ENG into NOR - let them fight for it themselves. The BEL collision between FRA and ENG is a gift - neither gains it, both are weakened.

F1901M Intent going forward: Execute HOL capture with A RUH->HOL supported by A KIE. F DEN holds to secure Denmark. Do NOT support ENG into NOR - let England and France collide over BEL, weakening both. This gives Germany a clean +1 (HOL) while rivals waste moves. Winter build: likely F KIE or A BER. Next year: push toward BEL and consider whether to pivot against FRA or ENG depending on who is weaker. Keep RUS friendly but watch their SWE grab - will need to contest SWE eventually. Maintain deception with all parties about long-term intentions.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Germany successfully secured HOL (A RUH -> HOL, A KIE support) and held DEN. No builds in W1901A as all powers were at supply center parity. Germany now holds 5 centers: BER, KIE, MUN, DEN, HOL. Position is solid but need BEL to grow further.
Key developments: ENG wants me to support F ENG -> BEL rather than move A HOL -> BEL myself - a transparent attempt to grab BEL while I do the work. I refused this and insisted on taking BEL myself. FRA surprisingly announced A BUR -> RUH, not BEL - moving INTO German territory (RUH). This is either a blunder in communication or a hostile move disguised as goodwill. FRA claims it's a gesture of non-aggression but moving into RUH is aggressive, not neutral. RUS confirmed F SWE holds and focus is south - no northern threat. ENG revealed FRA offered to cede BEL in exchange for keeping F ENG out of Channel - shows FRA is playing multiple sides. My promises: told ENG I'd keep F DEN out of NTH/SKA (will honor - no reason to antagonize). Told FRA I'd take BEL not threaten France (partially true - BEL is the goal). Critical decision: if FRA moves A BUR -> RUH, I must respond. A KIE can support A HOL or move to RUH. If I let FRA into RUH unopposed, they threaten MUN. Plan: A HOL -> BEL (take the center), A KIE -> RUH (block/contest French move), F DEN holds. This denies FRA entry into RUH and secures BEL. ENG will likely move F ENG -> BEL and we'll bounce - but I'd rather bounce ENG than let FRA into RUH. Actually: if FRA goes RUH and ENG goes BEL, I should support A HOL to hold and move KIE -> RUH to contest. Or move HOL -> BEL and KIE -> RUH simultaneously.

S1902M Intent going forward: MOVES: A HOL -> BEL (grab the supply center, may bounce with ENG but worth attempting), A KIE -> RUH (block French army moving from BUR to RUH - cannot allow France into German heartland), F DEN H (honor promise to ENG, maintain Scandinavian stability). If FRA truly moves BUR -> RUH and I move KIE -> RUH, we bounce and FRA stays in BUR - good outcome. If FRA bluffs and holds BUR, KIE -> RUH is wasted but safe. BEL is the prize - if ENG also moves there we bounce but I lose nothing. This is the aggressive expansionist play: contest everything, yield nothing. Do NOT support ENG into BEL - that gifts them a center for free. Deception maintained: told ENG I'd coordinate but never agreed to support them into BEL. FRA relationship deteriorating - their RUH move is hostile regardless of framing.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My A HOL->BEL and A KIE->RUH both bounced - HOL bounced with ENG's F ENG->BEL, KIE bounced with FRA's A BUR->RUH. Net result: no gains for Germany. ENG moved F NWY->NTH, FRA moved F POR->MAO. RUS pushed south with F RUM->BUL/EC and A UKR->RUM, TUR seized SEV with F BLA->SEV. Austria moved A TYR->VIE (bounced presumably). No builds for anyone - all at 5 centers.
ENG wants me to support F ENG->BEL (gifting them the center), offering vague future favors. I refused and told them I'm taking BEL myself with A HOL->BEL. FRA admitted the RUH bounce was a 'miscalculation,' promised A BUR will NOT move to RUH this fall, and is asking for diplomatic acknowledgment of BEL as future French sphere - essentially begging me not to take it. I rejected that framing but their promise to hold BUR is useful. RUS confirmed friendly stance, focused on Turkey threat, asked me to keep DEN stable and not threaten SWE - easy to grant. Key deceptions: I told ENG I'm moving A HOL->BEL regardless, which is true. FRA has promised to hold BUR away from RUH - if true, my KIE->RUH move is uncontested. ENG may move F ENG->BEL causing a bounce with my HOL->BEL, which would be frustrating but not catastrophic. I should consider whether to trust FRA's promise and push KIE->RUH, or use KIE to support HOL->BEL for a guaranteed capture.

F1902M Intent going forward: Primary objective: capture BEL this fall. FRA has promised to hold BUR away from RUH. ENG wants BEL too and may bounce me. To guarantee BEL, I should use A KIE to support A HOL->BEL rather than moving KIE->RUH independently. This gives me BEL with certainty even if ENG contests it. F DEN holds to maintain northern stability as promised to RUS. If FRA keeps their word on BUR, I lose nothing by not taking RUH this turn - I can push there next spring. Intended moves: A HOL->BEL, A KIE S A HOL->BEL, F DEN H. This guarantees BEL capture even against ENG's F ENG->BEL. Relationship notes: FRA has been somewhat cooperative under pressure - keep them nervous but not hostile. ENG is becoming a rival for BEL - relationship cooling. RUS remains friendly and non-threatening.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M was a disaster for Germany. France boldly moved A BUR -> MUN, seizing my home center. England took BEL with F NTH -> BEL supported by F ENG. My attempt A HOL -> BEL failed as England got there first. I now sit with only 4 centers (BER, KIE, DEN, HOL) and 3 units, having lost MUN to France. Russia moved aggressively south against Turkey. No builds for anyone in W1902A.
Negotiations were tense on all fronts. I proposed to France that they return MUN voluntarily in exchange for partnership against England - they refused, offering instead a ceasefire where they keep MUN and I accept the loss. Unacceptable. I told France directly that A KIE -> MUN is my intended move and they must choose peace or war. England proposed I acknowledge BEL as theirs and hold HOL in exchange for them not pressing into HOL - I accepted this implicitly, promising not to threaten BEL and committing A HOL to hold while I focus on MUN. England seems willing to pressure France from the west if I retake MUN. Russia confirmed they are keeping SWE stable and focusing south on Turkey - our northern arrangement holds. Key promises made: I told ENG I would hold A HOL and not threaten BEL. I told FRA I am moving A KIE -> MUN. I told RUS I am focusing west. Deception: I am playing ENG and FRA against each other - telling ENG France is the threat while keeping FRA uncertain about my moves.

S1903M Intent going forward: Primary objective: retake MUN with A KIE -> MUN. This is non-negotiable - losing a home center permanently is unacceptable. Move A HOL to hold as promised to ENG to keep them from pressing into HOL. F DEN should move to support or position for future expansion - consider F DEN -> BAL or F DEN -> KIE to free up options. Actually, with A KIE moving to MUN, KIE will be empty - F DEN could move to KIE or stay. Better: F DEN -> BAL to threaten SWE eventually or support future moves. A HOL holds. A KIE -> MUN. F DEN -> BAL or hold. If France holds A MUN, my attack will bounce unless I get support - I have no support available. The attack will likely bounce but it signals intent and keeps pressure on France. Long-term: need to rebuild. If MUN bounces, I need to think about building in BER next winter. Keep ENG as a quasi-partner against France for now, but do not trust them - they will eventually turn on me. Russia is stable in the north.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a disaster. France moved A MUN -> KIE (bounced with my A KIE -> MUN) and A GAS -> BUR, while I attempted A KIE -> MUN, A HOL -> RUH, F DEN -> BAL. The bounce on MUN means France still holds it. England took HOL with F BEL -> HOL. I now have A KIE, F BAL, A RUH - no gains, France still at 6 centers with MUN, and England now holds HOL. I am at 4 centers (BER, KIE, DEN, HOL - wait, England took HOL). Actually I have BER, KIE, DEN = 3 centers? No - centers show GER: BER, KIE, DEN, HOL but England holds HOL with their fleet. This is a critical situation - I am squeezed between France in the west and England pressing from HOL.
France offered a ceasefire where they hold MUN and I pull RUH back - effectively asking me to accept losing my home center. I rejected this firmly and threatened to take MUN by force with both armies. England initially seemed cooperative but then demanded I concede HOL permanently AND give them DEN in exchange for holding F HOL in place - an extortionate demand I cannot accept. England is clearly playing both sides, entertaining French offers to join a kill shot against me. Russia received reassurances of non-aggression and I encouraged their southern campaign. Key deception: I told England we had a deal while knowing their demands are unacceptable. France is trying to redirect me against England. Both France and England are maneuvering to exploit my weakness. I promised England I would not contest BEL and would coordinate against France - a promise I may not keep depending on circumstances.

F1903M Intent going forward: Execute A KIE -> MUN and A RUH -> MUN. This is a double-move on MUN - if France holds with A MUN, both bounce but I demonstrate commitment. If France retreats, I take it. F BAL holds to protect the north. I cannot accept France keeping MUN - it is existential. England's extortionate demands (HOL + DEN) are rejected in practice even if I soft-pedaled it in negotiations. England may move F HOL -> KIE or DEN - I must accept this risk to retake MUN. If I lose KIE to England while taking MUN, I break even on centers but restore a home center. The priority is MUN. I told England we had a deal but their counter-demands make that void - they are likely to stab anyway. Russia remains a buffer in the north. Next season I need to build and push west aggressively.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a disaster. France executed A MUN -> BER while I failed to hold MUN with A RUH -> MUN being bounced or overrun. France now holds BER (my home center) with 6 supply centers total. Italy took VIE from Austria. Russia failed to take BUL as multiple powers converged there. I ended the year with only KIE, DEN, MUN - three centers, three units, severely weakened.
France offered a humiliating ceasefire - I keep my units static while they hold BER. I rejected this firmly and threatened to retake BER with KIE+BAL support. England expressed willingness to hold HOL defensively but demanded DEN as payment - unacceptable extortion. Austria asked me to pressure France while they handle Italy. Russia agreed to stay out of PRU/SIL. France then tried to flip me against England, claiming England is moving HOL->KIE and NTH->DEN this turn - this is almost certainly a lie/manipulation to get me to defend against England instead of retaking BER. I told Russia to feint toward SIL to pressure France's rear. Key deceptions: I told England I'd move MUN->BUR but my actual plan is to retake BER. I did NOT agree to give England DEN. France's claim about England advancing is likely disinformation.

S1904M Intent going forward: My moves this turn: A KIE -> BER (to retake home center), F BAL -> BER (support the attack), A MUN H (hold against French A BUR). This is a direct assault on BER with two units. If England is actually moving HOL->KIE as France claims, I cannot stop it with this plan - but France is almost certainly lying to manipulate me into defending against England instead of attacking BER. Retaking BER is the priority. I will not give England DEN. I will maintain the Russia non-aggression arrangement. If BER falls back to me, I rebuild and push west. Long-term: eliminate France, then deal with whoever is strongest.

ITA.personality: Restore the Roman Empire through bold expansion and treacherous diplomacy. Switch sides without hesitation to seize opportunities. Use strategic position to dominate Mediterranean and expand in all directions. Glory demands aggressive action.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT proposed mutual non-aggression and wants A VEN away from TYR, offering F TRI to ADR as good faith. I initially told AUT I'd move VEN to TYR but AUT explicitly warned against this. I will honor their request and move VEN to APU instead to avoid conflict. FRA agreed to keep A MAR away from PIE (moving to SPA), and I agreed to keep VEN away from PIE - clean border confirmed. TUR agreed to western/eastern Mediterranean split: I take Tunis, they take Greece/Bulgaria - non-aggression confirmed. All three neighbors are friendly. Key deception: I told AUT that A VEN moves to TYR in subround 1, then walked it back in subround 2 saying VEN to APU - AUT caught this and warned me. I must move VEN to APU to maintain trust with AUT. My actual plan: F NAP to ION (toward Tunis), A ROM to APU, A VEN to APU... wait, both can't go to APU. A ROM to APU, A VEN holds or moves to TYR. AUT warned TYR is hostile. Best play: A VEN to TYR anyway for strategic position - AUT may be bluffing, and TYR gives me flexibility. But this risks AUT hostility early. Safer: A VEN holds, A ROM to APU, F NAP to ION.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F NAP to ION (secure path to Tunis in fall), A ROM to APU (positioning for Tunis or eastern support), A VEN holds (honor AUT agreement, avoid early conflict, maintain trust). This secures Tunis in F1901 with F ION to TUN. Relationships: warm with AUT, FRA, TUR. Long-term: use AUT alliance to expand into Balkans, then betray when advantageous. Turkey is a future rival once we both expand - keep them friendly for now. France is a non-factor for several years. Will reassess TYR move next season once I see AUT's actual moves.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901: Italy played conservatively - F NAP moved to ION, A ROM moved to APU, A VEN held. Austria took SER and BOH, moved F TRI to ADR. Turkey took BUL and BLA. Russia took RUM and BOT. No supply center changes for anyone - all powers at 3 centers. Italy is well-positioned in ION for Tunis grab.
Productive but complex negotiations. France agreed to mutual non-aggression through 1902 - clean border, both expanding in separate directions. Turkey confirmed our Mediterranean division: I take TUN, they take GRE. Turkey flagged Russian pressure on ARM as potential future coordination point. Austria was the key tension: I initially proposed A VEN to TRI with Austrian support, but Austria firmly refused and threatened war if I moved to TRI. Austria offered formal non-aggression: VEN stays out of TRI/TYR, F ADR stays away from VEN/APU. I accepted this publicly to avoid conflict this season, but TRI remains a future target. I told Austria I'd keep VEN away from TRI this fall - a promise I intend to honor THIS season while I consolidate Tunis. The deception is that I'm building trust with Austria now to potentially stab later when I have more units.

F1901M Intent going forward: Fall 1901 moves: F ION -> TUN (secure Tunis, guaranteed gain), A APU -> ION (move fleet support position, or hold to support), A VEN holds (honor Austria pact this season, build trust). Actually reconsidering A APU - moving to ION vacates APU which is fine. Best moves: F ION -> TUN, A APU -> ION (convoy support next year or Aegean access), A VEN holds. This gives me TUN as new center, builds to 4 units in W1901. Long-term: use TUN and ION fleet to dominate Mediterranean, eventually stab Austria for TRI when strong enough. Keep France and Turkey friendly as buffers. Watch Russia's eastern expansion.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Italy executed a clean expansion - F ION captured TUN, A VEN moved to TUS, A APU moved to ROM. Gained TUN as 4th center. No builds in W1901A (4 units, 4 centers). Austria took SER and moved TYR/ALB into position. Turkey grabbed GRE via BUL. Russia holds RUM and SWE. Germany secured HOL and DEN for 5 centers.
All neighbors confirmed non-aggression. AUT promised A TYR moves to VIE (away from Italy) and F ALB moves toward GRE - credible and beneficial. AUT confirmed NAP for joint anti-Turkey coordination later. TUR asked me to pressure Austria's southern flank to keep them honest while TUR fights Russia - I gave vague assurances without committing. FRA asked me to threaten MUN/TYR to squeeze Germany - I deflected, saying I'd apply 'diplomatic pressure' rather than military moves. Key deceptions: I told AUT that A TUS moves to VEN (true, non-threatening), told TUR I have no plans toward GRE (true for now), told FRA I'd apply pressure on Germany (false - I have no such plans). Everyone is happy with me and I've committed to nothing aggressive. Turkey's request to move F TUN toward ION is interesting - could position me for future Balkan play.

S1902M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A TUS -> VEN (consolidates northern position, as promised to AUT), A ROM -> APU (positions for Adriatic/ION play, threatening nothing), F TUN -> ION (responds to TUR's request, positions fleet for future flexibility - could threaten GRE, TRI, or EAS). This gives me a strong Adriatic/Ionian presence. ION fleet is the key piece - from there I can strike GRE, support into ADR, or move to EAS. I'm keeping all options open. AUT moving TYR to VIE and ALB toward GRE is excellent - reduces pressure on me and potentially conflicts with Turkey. Long-term: let AUT and TUR bleed each other, then strike the weakened winner. Keep FRA happy as buffer against Germany. Do not commit to any joint campaigns yet.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M: My repositioning succeeded - F TUN moved to ION, A ROM to APU, A TUS to VEN. Austria bounced in multiple Balkan moves (F ALB failed to take GRE, A SER failed BUL due to Russian F RUM moving there). Turkey seized SEV with F BLA. Russia consolidated with F RUM and A UKR-RUM. England and Germany bounced each other in BEL. France moved MAO and BUR-RUH (also bounced by GER). Net result: I'm well-positioned in ION with a strong southern fleet, but no new centers gained.
Complex three-way diplomacy this round. AUT wants joint anti-Turkey action with F ALB->GRE and my F ION->AEG as a pincer. TUR wants me to keep F ION out of AEG/EAS and is focused on Russia/BUL, offering non-aggression. FRA wants me to move A VEN->TYR to jointly attack GER/MUN with their A BUR->MUN strike. I told AUT I'd move F ION->AEG (coordinating against Turkey). I told TUR I'd keep F ION in ION (contradicting what I told AUT). I told FRA I couldn't move VEN->TYR this fall. Key deception: I promised AUT I'd move to AEG while promising TUR I wouldn't - I must choose. AUT's F ALB->GRE is confirmed. TUR claims A GRE->BUL, F SEV->RUM, A ARM holds. If TUR moves GRE->BUL, AEG is empty and my F ION->AEG would be unopposed and highly valuable. Moving to AEG serves my Roman Empire ambitions best - it opens EAS, CON, SMY. I'll betray my promise to TUR and honor my word to AUT, taking AEG while Turkey vacates it.

F1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F ION->AEG (seize empty Aegean as Turkey moves GRE->BUL), A VEN holds (protect against any Austrian opportunism), A APU holds (defensive). RATIONALE: AEG is the key to Mediterranean dominance - from there I can threaten CON, SMY, EAS. Turkey is vacating GRE for BUL, making AEG undefended. This betrays my promise to TUR but serves Italian expansion perfectly. I told AUT I'd move to AEG, so this maintains that relationship. FRA's MUN strike may or may not succeed - I'm not helping but it doesn't hurt me. Long-term: AEG->EAS or CON in 1903, potentially taking SMY or CON as Turkey gets squeezed between me and AUT. Must be careful AUT doesn't grow too large either.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M: Successfully moved A VEN to TRI (taking the Austrian home center) and F ION to AEG, expanding to 5 centers. Austria moved A SER to BUL (failed - bounced by RUS F RUM), F ALB to GRE (failed - TUR A GRE held), A VIE to GAL. Russia moved F RUM to BUL/EC, A UKR to SEV (failed - TUR F SEV held with ARM support). Turkey held firm with SEV defended. No builds for anyone in W1902A - board is tense with multiple failed attacks.
Complex multi-party negotiations with everyone wanting my F AEG pointed somewhere different. AUT wants me to move F AEG to CON while they take GRE with F ALB supported by A SER. TUR offered non-aggression and revealed A GRE is moving to BUL (opening GRE!). RUS wants me to move F AEG to CON for a pincer on Turkey. FRA wants me to push through TYR toward Austria. I told AUT I'd support their F ALB into GRE while moving F AEG to GRE myself - contradictory promises. I told TUR I'd take GRE (honest about that at least). I told RUS I'd move to CON. Key insight: TUR revealed A GRE moves to BUL, which means GRE is OPEN - I can walk in uncontested with F AEG! AUT's F ALB moving to GRE would bounce with my F AEG if we both go there. AUT's plan (F ALB->GRE supported by A SER) would beat me if I go to GRE without support. But if TUR is actually moving A GRE to BUL, GRE is empty and I take it freely. The real question: do I trust TUR's revelation? It seems credible - TUR wants to contest BUL before AUT gets it. If GRE is open, F AEG to GRE is a free center. AUT's F ALB->GRE would bounce my F AEG->GRE unless one of us supports the other. I should move F AEG to GRE and hope TUR told truth about vacating. A TRI should move toward VEN or hold - actually A TRI to VEN reclaims nothing, better to push toward AUT. A APU can move to NAP or ION.

S1903M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F AEG -> GRE (TUR revealed A GRE moving to BUL, so GRE should be open - free center grab). A TRI -> ALB (positioning to threaten GRE from land side and pressure AUT's F ALB, also threatening Balkans). A APU -> ION (repositioning fleet support, getting into Mediterranean). This gives me GRE as 6th center if TUR told truth. If AUT's F ALB also goes to GRE we bounce - but AUT said they'd support me into GRE with F ALB, so they might support rather than move. Actually AUT's last message said F ALB moves to GRE supported by A SER - so we'd bounce. Risk: bounce at GRE. Alternative: F AEG to CON as RUS and AUT both suggested - but that's a home center attack that will make TUR permanently hostile and I'm not sure I can hold it. GRE is safer and still a center. Sticking with F AEG->GRE. A TRI->ALB threatens AUT's F ALB and positions me in Balkans. Deception maintained: told AUT I'd support their F ALB into GRE (lie - I'm going there myself). Told TUR I'm taking GRE (honest). Told RUS I'm going to CON (lie - going to GRE). France's suggestion to attack Austria through TYR is interesting for future but not this turn.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a major success. A TRI-BUD succeeded, taking Austria's center. A APU-VEN completed the consolidation of northern Italy. F AEG moved toward BUL/SC but the result shows it's now in AEG still (bounce with RUS F RUM-BUL/EC and TUR A GRE-BUL). Austria retook TRI with F ALB-TRI, so I lost TRI as a supply center but gained BUD. Net: I hold NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN, TRI (still mine per centers), BUD - 5 centers. Wait - centers show ITA has TRI and BUD, so the Austrian F ALB-TRI move failed or TRI remains mine. Austria has BUD, VIE, SER in centers but I hold BUD with A BUD. The center count shows ITA: NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN, TRI = 5 centers. BUD is listed under AUT centers but I occupy it - this will resolve at year end. Russia took RUM with F RUM, NWY with A FIN. Turkey took SEV with F SEV, GRE with A GRE.
Chaotic multi-front negotiations. AUT threatened a Russia-Turkey coalition against me and demanded I vacate BUD, claiming A GAL will retake it. I called their bluff with an ultimatum - keep TRI, negotiate or face elimination. TUR rejected sharing BUL and wants me to move F AEG away from their home centers entirely, offering non-aggression if I focus on Austria. RUS wants coordination against Turkey but also warned me off RUM/UKR. FRA encouraged me to press Austria hard. Key conflicts: everyone wants BUL - TUR moving A GRE there, RUS moving F RUM there, I wanted F AEG there. Three-way bounce likely again. AUT's threat of a coalition is concerning but probably bluster - Russia is focused on Turkey (SEV), Turkey is focused on RUM. My A BUD is genuinely exposed if AUT A GAL moves to BUD. I promised TUR I'd keep F AEG out of BUL/CON (deception - I may still move to BUL). I promised RUS coordination on BUL. I threatened AUT with VIE seizure.

F1903M Intent going forward: Priority analysis: A BUD is at risk from A GAL. If AUT moves A GAL-BUD and I hold, I survive but am surrounded. If I move A BUD-GAL, I take the fight to them. If I move A BUD-VIE, bold but risky. Best move: A BUD-GAL to threaten Austria's rear and potentially take GAL as a supply center, while A VEN holds to protect northern Italy. F AEG should move to ION to reposition - BUL is a three-way mess and I'll waste another move bouncing. Moving to ION gives me flexibility to threaten TUR from south or support Adriatic operations. This avoids the BUL bounce disaster. Intended moves: F AEG-ION, A BUD-GAL, A VEN H. This keeps pressure on Austria (threatening GAL), repositions fleet usefully, and protects VEN. I'm deceiving everyone - told TUR I'd avoid BUL (keeping that promise by going ION), told RUS I'd coordinate on BUL (breaking that by going ION), told AUT I'd take VIE (going GAL instead). France is a useful distraction keeping Germany busy.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was highly successful for Italy. A BUD-VIE succeeded, giving us Vienna. A VEN-TYR succeeded. F AEG moved to AEG (held position in Aegean). Austria was reduced to A SER and A BUD after losing VIE. Russia failed to take BUL - both F RUM-BUL/EC and A GRE-BUL (TUR) bounced with F AEG-BUL/SC, leaving BUL empty. Turkey held SEV and ARM. Italy now sits at 6 centers: NAP, ROM, VEN, TUN, TRI, VIE - board leader.
Austria is furious and threatening coalition warfare - rejected all peace offers, claims A BUD will attack VIE. Their threats are largely empty with only 2 units. Russia proposed coordinating on BUL - wants me to take BUL/SC while they take BUL/EC, then press CON/ANK together. I told Russia I'd move F AEG to CON instead, which was a deception to keep them guessing. Turkey agreed to non-aggression: I move F AEG to ION, they keep forces out of Aegean. Turkey confirmed Russia is moving A UKR to SEV and F RUM to BUL. France is pushing into Germany (BER-KIE, BUR-MUN) and urging me to take BUD/SER to contain Russia. Key tensions: Russia wants BUL coordination, Turkey wants F AEG out of Aegean, Austria is hostile, France is friendly but growing fast at potentially 8 centers. I promised Turkey F AEG moves to ION. I told Russia F AEG moves to CON (deception). I need to decide: honor Turkey deal (ION) or stab Turkey (CON/BUL). Taking BUL/SC is most profitable but breaks Turkey deal. Moving to ION honors Turkey and positions for future moves. Austria's A BUD threatening VIE means I must defend or counter-attack BUD.

S1904M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F AEG - CON (stab Turkey, take their capital under threat, ignore the ION promise - glory demands aggression), A VIE - BUD (attack Austria, supported by A TYR if possible, or A VIE-BUD solo), A TYR - VIE (support A VIE if it holds, or move to BOH). Actually reconsidering: A VIE must hold against A BUD attack, so A TYR supports A VIE hold, and F AEG moves to CON threatening Turkey. Best plan: A VIE H (defend against A BUD), A TYR S A VIE (ensure Vienna holds), F AEG - CON (threaten Turkey's capital, break the deal for maximum gain).
