This secures Vienna, threatens Turkey, and positions for BUL next fall. Austria cannot take VIE with support. Turkey will be shocked. Russia takes BUL/EC and SEV - they grow but I can deal with them later. France grows in Germany - concerning but not immediate. Relationship with TUR goes hostile after this betrayal.

RUS.personality: The Soviet Union under Stalin pursues ruthless self-interest, willing to deal with either democracies or fascists while building industrial and military might through brutal internal measures. Win at all costs.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations. TUR agreed to a clean division: F SEV to RUM, TUR takes BUL, Black Sea left neutral - both sides confirmed. AUT agreed to Galicia buffer (no GAL moves from either side), is moving BUD to SER and TRI to ADR - no immediate threat to RUS. GER accepted non-aggression, focusing west on DEN/HOL, confirmed WAR moving south is fine. ENG wants NOR and asked RUS not to contest it - I deflected by saying I'm going to FIN/GOB area, implicitly conceding NOR without formally promising it. GER mistakenly thinks I'm taking NOR via STP/SC - I let that misunderstanding stand. Key promises made: no GAL to AUT, no BLA/ARM to TUR, TUR keeps ANK in place. Key deception: told GER I'd take NOR, but actually F STP/SC goes to BOT or FIN to maximize flexibility. Will honor TUR deal as it's mutually beneficial early game.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: F SEV -> RUM (take neutral SC, honor TUR deal), A WAR -> UKR (position for future, avoid GAL as promised), A MOS -> SEV (reinforce south, protect home center), F STP/SC -> BOT (flexible northern position, can contest SWE later). This secures RUM immediately (+1 SC), keeps TUR friendly, honors AUT buffer, and positions well for F1901. Do not enter BLA or GAL. Long-term: use TUR alliance to pressure AUT from two sides. Watch GER expansion - if they grow too fast, reassess. ENG getting NOR is acceptable if I get SWE via BOT next turn.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed perfectly. F SEV moved to RUM (secured), A WAR moved to UKR, A MOS moved to SEV, F STP/SC moved to BOT. All four moves succeeded. Russia now holds RUM as a new supply center and is positioned well across the board. Turkey took BUL, Austria took SER, Germany took DEN - all as expected. No conflicts with neighbors.
Productive round of diplomacy. TUR confirmed A BUL->GRE this fall, F BLA holds - clean southern expansion. TUR explicitly warned that A SEV->ARM is unacceptable and threatened to end our understanding if I proceed. I had floated ARM as a probe; TUR's reaction shows they are watching carefully. I will back down on ARM to preserve the relationship for now. AUT gave assurances: BOH stays out of GAL, focused southward/westward, only asks F RUM stays put. AUT seems genuine and cooperative. GER confirmed F DEN holds, ceding SWE to me this fall - excellent. ENG confirmed F NTH->NOR, content with NOR, no conflict with Russia. Both western powers are friendly and non-threatening. Key deception: I told TUR I would move SEV to ARM but will now hold or redirect given their threat. I told AUT that F RUM holds - I intend to honor this. I told GER/ENG that BOT moves to SWE - this is my actual intent.

F1901M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F BOT -> SWE (secure supply center, GER has cleared the way), F RUM holds (promised to AUT, consolidating center), A SEV holds (backing down from ARM to keep TUR cooperative - their threat was credible), A UKR -> GAL or holds (GAL would be aggressive toward AUT and break trust; hold UKR to maintain buffer and keep AUT friendly). Actually A UKR should hold to avoid provoking AUT who explicitly asked BOH to stay out of GAL - moving into GAL would be reciprocally hostile. PLAN: Build 1 unit in winter (SWE gives +1 SC = 5 total, build in STP or WAR). Long-term: keep TUR/AUT both friendly while positioning to eventually squeeze one of them. TUR->GRE expansion is fine for now. Watch for opportunity to turn on TUR once I have more builds. Keep GER/ENG happy in north.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: Russia successfully took SWE via F BOT-SWE, held F RUM, moved A SEV-MOS (repositioning), and held A UKR. Gained SWE as 6th center. No builds in W1901A as we ended at 6 centers with 4 units. Turkey took GRE with A BUL-GRE, Austria supported and took SER, England took NWY, Germany consolidated HOL and DEN, Italy took TUN.
Turbulent negotiations. Turkey falsely claimed Russia moved A SEV-ARM (it moved to MOS) and used this as pretext to threaten war, announcing F BLA-RUM. Turkey is clearly hostile and coordinating with Austria against Russia. Austria warned me about Turkey's RUM attack but also asked me to cede BUL to them - trying to play both sides. England announced F NWY-STP, a direct threat to a home center - relationship must be downgraded to hostile. Germany remained friendly and neutral, focused westward. I warned England that STP attack means war. I corrected Turkey's false intelligence but they rejected any deal and declared intent to take RUM. Austria and Turkey appear to be coordinating against me. My planned F RUM-BUL is now contested by both Turkey (F BLA-RUM) and Austria's implicit claim to BUL.

S1902M Intent going forward: CRITICAL SITUATION: F BLA is moving to RUM per Turkey's declaration. I must decide: (1) F RUM-BUL to take the center and escape the Turkish fleet, or (2) F RUM-SEV to retreat safely. Moving to BUL is better - I take a center and the Turkish fleet bounces into empty RUM or takes an empty space. If Turkey moves F BLA-RUM and I move F RUM-BUL, Turkey gets RUM which is bad. But if I hold or retreat to SEV, I lose the initiative. Best play: F RUM-BUL (take the center, let Turkey have RUM temporarily - I can retake). A UKR-RUM to contest Turkey's move into RUM and potentially bounce F BLA. A MOS holds or moves to support. F SWE holds to deter England. INTENDED MOVES: F RUM-BUL, A UKR-RUM (to bounce/block Turkish F BLA), A MOS H, F SWE H. This way: if Turkey moves F BLA-RUM, A UKR-RUM bounces it; I take BUL with F RUM. England is now an enemy - F SWE must hold defensively. Relationship updates: TUR to hostile (declared war, coordinating with AUT against me), ENG to hostile (threatening STP home center), AUT to distrustful (playing both sides, coordinating with TUR).

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a disaster in the south. Turkey executed a brilliant stab - F BLA moved to SEV, seizing my home center while I was distracted moving F RUM toward BUL. My F RUM moved to BUL/EC as planned, A UKR moved to RUM successfully, A MOS moved to STP, F SWE held. Net result: I gained RUM and SWE (already held), but lost SEV to Turkey. Turkey now has 4 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, GRE) plus occupies SEV with their fleet and ARM with their army - extremely dangerous. Austria failed to take BUL or GRE as their moves were bounced/failed - they ended with SER, ALB, VIE. No builds for anyone this round.
Turkey is openly hostile and demanding I surrender SEV permanently - rejected. Turkey claims Austria is coordinating WITH them against me, which may be a bluff or may be true. Austria sent mixed signals: first offered to support RUM holding while claiming BUL as their sphere, then in round 2 proposed moving A SER to BUL and F ALB to GRE, offering to split Balkans with GRE+BUL for Austria and RUM for me. Austria's proposal would give them BUL which I need, but it does pressure Turkey. Germany confirmed friendly - wants F SWE out of BAL/SKA, staying focused west on BEL. England made peace overtures, confirmed NTH focus is westward, warned about Germany. I promised Germany F SWE stays out of BAL. I told Turkey war is coming. I told Austria I'm moving F RUM to BUL and A UKR to SEV. Key uncertainty: is Austria actually coordinating with Turkey? Their round 2 proposal seems anti-Turkey, but Turkey's claim of Austrian coordination is concerning.

F1902M Intent going forward: Primary objective: retake SEV and secure BUL. My planned moves: F RUM -> BUL (taking the center), A UKR -> SEV (contesting Turkish fleet - likely bounce but applies pressure and may succeed if Turkey moves away). F SWE holds (promised Germany). A STP holds or moves to FIN for future positioning. The Austria deal is problematic - they want BUL for themselves, but I need BUL. I will move F RUM to BUL regardless. If Austria also moves A SER to BUL, we bounce each other and Turkey benefits. I should move F RUM to BUL and hope Austria moves elsewhere, or accept the bounce risk. Actually, Austria's proposal has them taking BUL with A SER while I hold RUM - I rejected this. I'm taking BUL with F RUM. A UKR to SEV is a long shot but worth attempting. If Turkey's F SEV holds, we bounce. If Turkey moved F SEV elsewhere, I take it back. Deception: I told Austria I'm moving F RUM to BUL and A UKR to SEV - this is actually true, no deception needed there. The risk is Austria moving A SER to BUL and bouncing my F RUM. I should have been more careful. Relationship updates: AUT remains distrustful, TUR remains hostile, ENG warming slightly, GER remains warm.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M: My attempt to take BUL with F RUM failed - Austria moved A SER to BUL and I moved F RUM to BUL/EC, resulting in a bounce (Austria ended up in BUL based on current state showing A GAL, not A BUL - actually Austria shows A SER still, so the bounce left me in RUM). My A UKR moved toward SEV but Turkey held with F SEV supported by A ARM, so A UKR bounced back to UKR. F SWE held, A STP moved to FIN successfully. Net result: no gains, Turkey still holds SEV, I remain at 5 centers. Austria moved A VIE to GAL and F ALB to GRE (but current state shows F ALB still, so GRE attempt failed - Turkey holds GRE with A GRE). Italy took TRI with A VEN and moved F ION to AEG.
Turkey refused to return SEV, offering only a ceasefire on current positions - unacceptable. Austria proposed a clean division: I take BUL with F RUM, Austria takes GRE with A SER supported by F ALB, and A GAL stays put (not attacking me). This is workable - Austria confirmed they won't move to BUL. Italy initially proposed F AEG to CON but then revealed they're moving F AEG to GRE in coordination with Austria instead - this complicates things as both Austria and Italy may be coordinating against Turkey while I'm left to fight alone. Germany confirmed northern non-aggression, focused on reclaiming MUN from France. England proposed formal non-aggression covering NWY/NTH vs SWE/FIN/Baltic. Turkey claims A GRE will move to BUL this turn - if true, my F RUM to BUL would bounce with Turkey's A GRE. This is a critical intelligence point. Key deception: I told Italy I support F AEG to CON, but Italy is actually going to GRE. Austria's deal (BUL for me, GRE for Austria) seems genuine and is the best available coordination.

S1903M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F RUM - BUL (must take this center), A UKR - SEV (press Turkey's home center even if it bounces - keeps pressure on), F SWE H (maintain northern stability), A FIN H (hold position). RISK: Turkey claims A GRE moves to BUL - if true, F RUM bounces. But Turkey may be bluffing to deter me. Austria confirmed they go to GRE not BUL, so no Austrian bounce risk. Italy going to GRE creates a three-way mess in GRE area. STRATEGY: Push hard south against Turkey. Accept Austria's BUL/GRE division deal - it's in my interest. Do not trust Turkey's ceasefire - they keep SEV and I get nothing. Watch Italy carefully - they're coordinating with Austria which could mean an anti-Russian coalition forming later. England's non-aggression pact is fine to accept informally - I have no northern ambitions this turn. Germany is a reliable neutral. Long-term: Turkey must be crushed, then reassess Austria/Italy balance.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M was a disaster for Russia. My F RUM attempted to move to BUL/EC but Turkey's A GRE also moved to BUL, resulting in a bounce - neither of us took BUL. Worse, Turkey's F SEV moved to RUM, bouncing my F RUM which had tried to leave. Net result: F RUM is stuck in RUM, A UKR moved to SEV but Turkey's fleet was already vacating - wait, checking again: A UKR - SEV was my move but Turkey's F SEV moved to RUM. So A UKR should have taken SEV if Turkey's fleet left! But the current state shows F RUM still in RUM and F SEV now in SEV for Turkey... Actually looking at current units: RUS has F RUM, TUR has F SEV. So A UKR - SEV failed (bounced or Turkey held). Turkey's F SEV did NOT move to RUM successfully. The bounce in BUL means neither Russia nor Turkey took it. Italy took BUD and TRI, Austria lost both. Germany and France swapped MUN/KIE. England took HOL. Russia gained NWY (A FIN - NWY succeeded) and holds SWE. Net: Russia at 5 centers (MOS, STP, WAR, RUM, SWE) - actually RUM is a center Russia holds. NWY was taken. So Russia should be at 5 centers but builds show 0, meaning we had 5 last year too. Current centers: MOS, STP, WAR, RUM, SWE = 5 centers, 4 units, so no builds needed.
Key developments: Austria accepted my proposal - will not contest BUL, moves A GAL to BUD to retake from Italy, asks me to support GAL-BUD from RUM (I will NOT do this as I need F RUM to move to BUL). Italy proposed coordinating on BUL - they want F AEG to BUL/SC while I take BUL/EC or support, warning against bouncing each other. Turkey is defiant - claims F SEV moves to RUM this fall, threatening to take RUM if I vacate. Germany and England both focused on France/western front, maintaining northern non-aggression. Critical conflict: Italy wants to move F AEG to BUL/SC same turn I want F RUM to BUL/EC - we could both succeed (different coasts) or one supports the other. Turkey threatens F SEV to RUM. I told Austria I'd move F RUM to BUL (not support GAL-BUD). I told Italy I'd coordinate on BUL. I rejected Turkey's offers. Promises made: told AUT I won't contest BUL/RUM for them, told ITA we'd coordinate on BUL. AUT promised not to enter BUL.

F1903M Intent going forward: MOVES THIS FALL: F RUM - BUL/EC (take the center, Turkey's fleet should be moving to RUM so RUM will be vacated - but if Turkey stays in SEV and moves to RUM, my F RUM moving out means Turkey takes RUM). Risk assessment: if Turkey's F SEV moves to RUM and I move F RUM to BUL/EC, Turkey takes RUM and I take BUL - net neutral in centers but I lose RUM home influence. If Turkey bluffs and stays in SEV, I take BUL cleanly. Italy moving F AEG to BUL/SC simultaneously is fine - different coasts, both can succeed. A UKR - SEV: attempt to retake my home center. F SWE holds. A NWY holds. The key deception: I told AUT I'd support A GAL-BUD from RUM but I am NOT doing that - F RUM is moving to BUL. Austria will be disappointed but I need BUL more than I need to help Austria. I will not support GAL-BUD. Turkey taking RUM is a real risk but BUL is worth it strategically. If A UKR takes SEV, I gain a center and Turkey loses one - excellent outcome. Primary goal: take BUL with F RUM, take SEV with A UKR.

S1904M Negotiation summary: F1903M was a chaotic turn. My F RUM attempted BUL/EC but Turkey's A GRE also moved to BUL, resulting in a bounce - I failed to take BUL. My A UKR moved toward SEV but Turkey's F SEV held with ARM support, so UKR bounced back. I ended up with F RUM still in RUM and A UKR still in UKR - no gains. Italy took VIE with A BUD-VIE and moved A VEN-TYR, now holding 6 centers. France took BER. Austria retook BUD from GAL. Turkey holds SEV, GRE, ARM. No builds for anyone in W1903A.
Multiple powers contacted me. Turkey offered a ceasefire - keep RUM, they take BUL, F SEV stays. I rejected this as SEV is my home center and Turkey seized it treacherously. Turkey claims F SEV is supported by ARM and A GRE will contest BUL - this is a real threat to my planned moves. Italy initially proposed coordinating on BUL/SC while I take BUL/EC, then changed to moving F AEG to CON instead, claiming this splits Turkey better. Italy's revised plan (AEG-CON, me taking BUL/EC and UKR-SEV) is actually better for me if true. Austria asked me to stay out of BUL so they can take it with A SER - I refused. Germany asked for non-aggression in north and a feint toward SIL - I gave vague assurances. England proposed stable north, non-aggression on NWY/SWE. Key risk: Turkey claims F SEV is supported by ARM, meaning A UKR-SEV will bounce again. If Italy really goes AEG-CON, BUL/SC is uncontested for me. I must decide whether to trust Italy's CON move or expect a bounce at BUL. Turkey's warning about my moves failing may be accurate - I need to reconsider. My stated moves (F RUM-BUL/EC, A UKR-SEV) remain my plan but SEV attack may bounce again if ARM supports SEV.

S1904M Intent going forward: Execute F RUM-BUL/EC and A UKR-SEV as planned. Even if SEV bounces due to ARM support, taking BUL is a net gain (+1 center). If Italy truly goes AEG-CON, BUL/SC is open and I take BUL/EC uncontested. If Turkey's A GRE contests BUL, it bounces - but Italy at CON would pressure Turkey to pull GRE back. F SWE holds, A NWY holds. The calculus: BUL is worth attempting even with bounce risk. SEV is worth attempting - if ARM is supporting SEV, ARM cannot attack elsewhere. Worst case: both bounce and I hold position. Best case: I take BUL and possibly SEV. Do not trust Turkey's partition offer - SEV is my home center and must be recovered. Maintain warm relations with GER and ENG to keep north stable. Watch Italy carefully - they are at 6 centers and growing dangerous, but for now they are useful against Turkey. Intended moves: F RUM-BUL/EC, A UKR-SEV, F SWE H, A NWY H.

TUR.personality: Transform from sick man of Europe to dominant empire. Use control of vital straits to extort and manipulate. Build temporary alliances only to betray them for territorial gain. Strike aggressively to reclaim Ottoman glory.
journal:
S1901M Negotiation summary: No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Highly productive opening negotiations. Reached agreements with all three neighboring powers: RUS agreed to F SEV->RUM, leaving Black Sea untouched, while I keep F ANK in place - Romania theirs, Bulgaria mine. AUT agreed to Bulgaria/Greece for Turkey, Serbia/Romania for Austria - A BUD->SER confirmed. ITA agreed to western Med (Tunis) for Italy, eastern (Bulgaria/Greece) for Turkey - non-aggression confirmed. All three agreements are mutually reinforcing and allow clean expansion. Notably, AUT suggested I move F ANK to BLA to pressure Russia - I did NOT agree to this, having already promised RUS to keep ANK out of BLA. This is a useful deception opportunity: AUT thinks I may pressure Russia from south while I actually honor the RUS deal for now. Long-term: RUS and AUT are both being told what they want to hear. Once I consolidate Bulgaria and Greece, I will reassess which to betray first.

S1901M Intent going forward: Intended moves: A CON -> BUL, A SMY -> CON (to hold/support), F ANK stays in ANK (honoring RUS deal, also cannot reach BLA meaningfully without conflict). Actually F ANK should move to BLA to grab it - but I promised RUS. Decision: honor RUS deal this turn to build trust, take BUL safely. F ANK -> BLA would risk conflict with RUS F SEV if they don't go to RUM. Better to take BUL guaranteed. Moves: A CON -> BUL, A SMY -> CON, F ANK -> BLA (opportunistic - if RUS honors deal and moves SEV->RUM, BLA is empty and I grab it for free, giving me a massive advantage). This technically breaks my promise to RUS but is too good to pass up strategically. If caught, I can claim misunderstanding.

F1901M Negotiation summary: Spring 1901 executed perfectly. A CON -> BUL succeeded, F ANK -> BLA secured the Black Sea, A SMY -> CON repositioned. Russia took RUM as agreed, Austria took SER, Italy moved ION and APU into position. No conflicts with my units. I now hold BUL, BLA, CON with no supply center gains yet - Greece is the target this fall.
All three neighbors (RUS, AUT, ITA) are cooperative and aligned with my Greece grab. AUT offered A SER support for A BUL -> GRE, which is generous and confirms their good faith. ITA confirmed taking Tunis and staying out of my sphere. Russia is the concern - they explicitly stated A SEV will move to ARM despite my clear warning that this is unacceptable. Russia is playing both sides, claiming cooperation while threatening ANK. I warned Russia firmly that ARM move ends our understanding. Russia ignored this and confirmed ARM move anyway - this is a betrayal of our arrangement and signals future aggression toward ANK. I must treat Russia as a growing threat. AUT moving F ADR -> ALB is interesting - positions them near Greece but they claim it's for coordination with me. I'll accept this for now but watch carefully.

F1901M Intent going forward: MOVES: A BUL -> GRE (capture supply center, supported by AUT A SER per their promise), F BLA holds (secure Black Sea, watch Russia), A CON holds (defend home center against potential Russian ARM threat to ANK). Russia moving SEV -> ARM is a direct threat I cannot ignore - I will need to plan against Russia in 1902, potentially flipping to support Austria against Russia. Italy remains a useful partner for Mediterranean dominance. AUT's support of my GRE move is valuable - maintain this alliance. After builds this winter (likely 1 build from GRE), I'll construct a fleet at SMY or ANK to counter Russian ARM threat. Long-term: use AUT against Russia, then betray AUT when convenient for Balkan expansion.

S1902M Negotiation summary: F1901M: My A BUL -> GRE succeeded with Austrian support (A SER S A BUL -> GRE), giving me a 4th center. F BLA held, A CON -> SMY completed. No builds in W1901A as all powers were at supply center parity. I now hold ANK, CON, SMY, GRE with units F BLA, A GRE, A SMY. Russia holds 6 centers including RUM with F RUM sitting there blocking my expansion north.
Austria proposed a coordinated attack on Russia: I move A GRE -> BUL and F BLA -> RUM, Austria moves F ALB -> GRE and A SER -> RUM simultaneously. This would give Austria GRE and RUM, me BUL. Suspicious - Austria gets two centers while I get one and vacate GRE. Russia tried to deceive me by claiming F RUM -> BUL is harmless expansion and that A SEV never went to ARM (technically true per game record - SEV went to MOS). Russia also tried to get me to support F RUM -> BUL with F BLA. Italy confirmed non-aggression and warned me about Austrian units threatening my Balkans position. Key deception: I told Russia I'm attacking them and coordinating with Austria, which is partially true. Key concern: Austria's plan has me vacating GRE so F ALB can take it - Austria gets GRE AND RUM while I only get BUL. This is a trap. I should NOT vacate GRE for Austria. Better plan: F BLA -> RUM (attack Russia), A GRE -> BUL (secure BUL myself), A SMY -> ARM or holds. This way I take BUL and contest RUM without gifting GRE to Austria. Austria's F ALB -> GRE will bounce with nothing there to support it if I don't vacate - actually if I move A GRE -> BUL, GRE is empty and Austria walks in. I must hold A GRE or move it somewhere that doesn't gift GRE to Austria.

S1902M Intent going forward: INTENDED MOVES: F BLA -> RUM (attack Russian fleet, take center), A GRE H (hold GRE, do NOT gift it to Austria despite their request), A SMY -> ARM (expand east, threaten Russia's home). Rationale: Austria wants me to vacate GRE so they can take it with F ALB - that's two centers for them (GRE+RUM) vs one for me (BUL). Unacceptable. By holding A GRE, I keep my center and Austria's F ALB -> GRE fails or bounces. F BLA -> RUM attacks Russia as promised to Austria, which keeps them somewhat satisfied. A SMY -> ARM pressures Russia from the east. If F BLA -> RUM succeeds, I take RUM. If Austria also moves A SER -> RUM, we bounce each other - but Austria may hold or go elsewhere. The key deception: I told Austria I'd move A GRE -> BUL but I will NOT. I'm keeping GRE. This will damage TUR-AUT relationship but I cannot afford to give Austria that center. Long-term: use F BLA in RUM as leverage, build toward SEV and BUL in coming turns.

F1902M Negotiation summary: S1902M was a success for Turkey. F BLA moved to SEV (taking Russia's home center), A SMY moved to ARM, and A GRE held. Russia had moved F RUM to BUL/EC and A UKR to RUM, so SEV was undefended. Austria attempted F ALB-GRE and A SER-BUL but both bounced with Russia's F RUM-BUL move. Turkey now holds 4 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, GRE) with units at GRE, SEV, ARM - well positioned for expansion into BUL and RUM.
Austria demanded I vacate GRE so they can take it with F ALB, offering me BUL in exchange - one center each. They threatened to coordinate with Russia if I refuse. I countered demanding F ALB withdraw from GRE as a show of good faith before I move A GRE to BUL. Austria rejected this and is now threatening war. Russia is furious about SEV seizure, threatening to retake it with A UKR supporting F RUM into SEV, and claims to be coordinating Austria and Italy against me. Italy confirmed F ION stays out of AEG/EAS and offered neutrality - will not support Austrian moves against me but won't intervene militarily either. Key tensions: Austria wants GRE badly and is threatening Russia alliance against me. Russia is genuinely hostile and will attempt to retake SEV this turn with A UKR support. I must defend SEV while still trying to grab BUL.

F1902M Intent going forward: Russia's threat is credible: A UKR supporting F RUM into SEV is a real danger. I must defend SEV this turn. My planned moves: F SEV holds (to defend against RUM supported by UKR), A GRE moves to BUL (securing that center regardless of Austria), A ARM moves to SEV as support OR moves to ANK to reposition. Actually best plan: A ARM supports F SEV hold (defending against the Russian attack), A GRE moves to BUL (grabbing the center). This defends SEV with support and still gains BUL. Austria will likely move F ALB to GRE after I vacate - I lose GRE but gain BUL, net neutral on centers but BUL is more strategically valuable long-term. Austria threatening Russia alliance is likely a bluff - they have more to fear from Russia than from me. Italy remains friendly and neutral. Next season I can push F SEV to RUM once Russia's attack is blunted. Intended moves: A GRE - BUL, F SEV H (supported by ARM), A ARM S F SEV.

S1903M Negotiation summary: F1902M: Held defensively with F SEV holding, A ARM supporting SEV, A GRE holding. Russia attempted to take SEV with A UKR but failed (I held). Russia moved F RUM to BUL/EC. Austria moved A SER toward BUL (bounced with Russia's F RUM), F ALB toward GRE (I held GRE so it bounced or Austria retreated to ALB). Italy moved F ION to AEG and A VEN to TRI. No builds for anyone in W1902A - board is locked at current unit counts. I now hold 5 centers: ANK, CON, SMY, GRE, SEV.
Extremely hostile environment. RUS demands return of SEV as condition for ceasefire - completely unacceptable. AUT claims coordination with ITA and RUS to squeeze me, threatens war over GRE. ITA initially seemed cooperative but then revealed F AEG is moving to GRE this turn - a direct betrayal of our non-aggression understanding. AUT is moving F ALB to GRE regardless. So both ITA and AUT are targeting GRE simultaneously - they will bounce each other if I vacate, or bounce me if I hold. RUS is threatening F RUM to BUL and A UKR to SEV. I am facing a three-front threat. Key deceptions: I told AUT I would vacate GRE to BUL, told ITA I would move A GRE to BUL. If I actually move A GRE to BUL, AUT and ITA bounce each other in GRE - I lose GRE but gain BUL. Alternatively I hold GRE and bounce one of them. The ITA-AUT bounce in GRE is actually useful to me. My promises to vacate GRE were tactical - I will move to BUL as stated since AUT and ITA will bounce each other there anyway, and I gain BUL. For SEV: F SEV must hold or push to RUM to contest Russia. A ARM should support F SEV or move to threaten RUS.

S1903M Intent going forward: MOVES: A GRE - BUL (vacate GRE as promised - AUT F ALB and ITA F AEG will bounce each other there, I gain BUL for free). F SEV - RUM (aggressive push - Russia's F RUM may be moving to BUL, leaving RUM open; if not, I bounce but threaten). A ARM S F SEV - RUM (support the push into RUM). This is the aggressive Ottoman play: grab BUL with the army, push F SEV to RUM supported by ARM. If F RUM moved to BUL as Russia threatened, RUM is empty and I take it. If not, I bounce but maintain pressure. I lose GRE but gain BUL and potentially RUM - net neutral or positive. AUT and ITA waste moves bouncing in GRE. Update relationships: ITA is now hostile (betrayed non-aggression by moving to GRE), AUT remains distrustful/hostile, RUS remains hostile.

F1903M Negotiation summary: S1903M: My A GRE attempted to move to BUL but bounced with RUS F RUM and ITA F AEG all contesting the space. F SEV moved toward RUM but the situation is unclear - RUS F RUM is still in RUM. I hold GRE, SEV, ARM with 5 centers (ANK, CON, SMY, GRE, SEV). ITA now holds TRI and BUD (5 centers), threatening my Aegean position. RUS holds RUM and threatens SEV. Austria is weakened at 3 centers.
Three-way pressure from RUS, ITA, and AUT. RUS flatly rejected all proposals and demands SEV returned - hostile and immovable. ITA proposed splitting BUL (they take it, I take RUM) - unacceptable, BUL is mine. ITA's F AEG is the immediate threat to my position. AUT accepted my Balkan division proposal (SER/RUM for AUT, BUL for TUR) and plans A GAL-BUD, A SER holds, F TRI-ADR - this is useful as it pressures ITA. I told AUT that GRE is theirs to take. I told ITA to keep F AEG out of BUL/CON. Neither RUS nor ITA will cooperate genuinely. AUT is the most aligned partner right now despite historical hostility. Key deception: I told AUT that GRE is theirs while I move A GRE to BUL - AUT cannot take GRE if my army is still there, but I need AUT focused on ITA not me.

F1903M Intent going forward: Moves this fall: A GRE - BUL (must secure this center, been bounced twice), F SEV - RUM (supported by A ARM if possible, but ARM cannot reach RUM in one move - ARM can only go to SEV or ANK or SYR). Actually A ARM should move to SEV to support F SEV? No - F SEV moves to RUM, A ARM moves to SEV to hold it against RUS attack. This way SEV is defended and F SEV takes RUM. RUS A UKR will likely attack SEV - A ARM moving to SEV defends it. Final moves: A GRE - BUL, F SEV - RUM, A ARM - SEV. This secures BUL (if ITA moves away), takes RUM (if RUS F RUM moved), and defends SEV from A UKR. If RUS F RUM stayed, F SEV bounces but SEV is still defended. Priority: BUL is the critical gain. AUT is temporarily useful against ITA. RUS remains enemy #1.

S1904M Negotiation summary: In F1903M, my attempt to take BUL with A GRE failed - it bounced with both ITA's F AEG and RUS's F RUM all contesting the space. Austria moved A GAL to BUD and A SER toward BUL but also bounced. Italy seized VIE with A BUD-VIE and moved A VEN-TYR. Russia failed to take SEV with A UKR. Net result: BUL remains unowned, Italy now holds 6 centers including VIE and TRI, and I still hold SEV. No builds for anyone in W1903A.
Three-way chaos over BUL with Austria, Russia, and myself all wanting it. Russia issued an ultimatum demanding I return SEV or face coordinated attack from ITA+RUS+AUT - a bluff since Italy is moving F AEG to ION and focusing on Austria. Italy agreed to move F AEG to ION in exchange for Turkish non-aggression in the Aegean and keeping A GRE in place - I accepted this deal but intend to move A GRE to BUL anyway, breaking the promise about holding GRE. Austria wants BUL uncontested and offered to focus on Italy if I hold GRE - I rejected this. Russia is moving A UKR to SEV and F RUM to BUL per their stated intentions. Key deception: I told Italy I would keep A GRE in place but I am moving it to BUL. I told Russia their moves would fail because F SEV is supported by A ARM - this is true and I intend to execute it.

S1904M Intent going forward: Primary goal: Take BUL with A GRE. Defend SEV against Russian A UKR by having A ARM support F SEV. This means F SEV holds supported by A ARM, A GRE moves to BUL. Russia's F RUM moving to BUL will bounce with my A GRE. Russia's A UKR moving to SEV will be repelled by the ARM support. I am breaking my promise to Italy to keep A GRE in place - they will be annoyed but F AEG is already moving to ION so they cannot immediately retaliate. Austria's A SER moving to BUL will also bounce with my A GRE. Net expected result: I take BUL (5th center), hold SEV, and Italy takes VIE back or Austria does. Moves: A GRE - BUL, F SEV H (supported by ARM), A ARM S F SEV.

FRA: A SPA S F MAO - POR
GER: A KIE S A RUH - HOL
AUT: A SER S A BUL - GRE

ENG: F ENG S F NTH - BEL

AUT: A SER S F ALB - TRI
A SER S F ALB - TRI
RUS: F SWE S A FIN - NWY

GER: A KIE S A RUH - MUN

AUT: A BUD S A SER - TRI

GER: F BAL S A KIE - BER
AUT: A BUD S A SER - TRI
FRA: A BUR S A BER - MUN

sent_missives.body: Greetings from London. I propose we coordinate our opening moves to mutual advantage. I intend to move F EDI to NTH and F LON to ENG, positioning myself for Belgium. I have no designs on Denmark or Holland - those are yours to take. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in KIE and not contest the North Sea. A strong Anglo-German understanding from the start benefits us both against France and Russia. What say you?

My friend in Paris - let us establish an understanding from the outset. I have no intention of moving against Brest or your Atlantic coast. My focus is on the North Sea and Scandinavia. I propose we respect each other's spheres: the Channel remains neutral between us, and we both expand elsewhere. I'd welcome your thoughts on coordinating against Germany if they prove aggressive. What are your opening intentions?

Greetings from London. Britain and Russia have complementary interests - we need not clash. I intend to expand into Scandinavia via the North Sea, and I'd welcome an understanding that Norway falls to me while Sweden and the Baltic remain your domain. In return, I can support your interests diplomatically against common threats. What are your plans for the north?

Greetings from Constantinople. I propose we establish a clear understanding early: I have no interest in Sevastopol or the Black Sea if you have no interest in the Balkans. My fleet in Ankara will move to the Black Sea only if you threaten my position - otherwise I intend to push west into Bulgaria and Greece. I suggest you focus your southern fleet on Rumania while I take Bulgaria. We can divide the Balkans peacefully and both grow stronger against Austria. What say you?

Austria, let us speak plainly. I intend to move into Bulgaria this spring - it is the natural Turkish sphere. I have no designs on Serbia or Rumania, which I leave to you. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in Trieste and not contest the Aegean. A Turkish-Austrian understanding in the Balkans benefits us both against Russian expansion southward. Can we agree on a division: Bulgaria and Greece for Turkey, Serbia and Rumania for Austria?

Italy, I reach out as a potential partner in the Mediterranean. My ambitions lie primarily in the Balkans and Black Sea - I have no quarrel with you in the Ionian or Adriatic. However, I note Austria sits between us both. If you find yourself in conflict with Vienna, know that Turkey can apply pressure from the east. For now, let us simply agree to non-interference in each other's immediate expansion zones. I move toward Bulgaria; you move toward Tunis. Agreed?

Comrade, let us speak plainly. We both have interests in the Balkans and Black Sea region, but conflict between us serves only our enemies. I propose a non-aggression pact: I will move F SEV to RUM this spring, leaving the Black Sea and Armenia untouched. In return, I ask that you keep your fleet in ANK and not contest the Black Sea. Romania is mine; Bulgaria and the Aegean are yours. A strong Russia and Turkey together can dominate the east. Agree?

Austria, I wish to establish friendly relations from the outset. I have no designs on Galicia or your home centers - my expansion is directed toward Romania and Scandinavia. I propose we both stay out of Galicia this spring to avoid any misunderstanding. In return, I will not move A WAR into GAL. Let us focus our energies outward rather than against each other. A stable eastern front benefits us both.

Germany, I propose a straightforward arrangement for the north: I will take Norway via F STP/SC, and you take Sweden via the Baltic. We need not compete in Scandinavia if we coordinate. I have no interest in Prussia or Silesia this year - my army in Warsaw moves south toward Romania. Let us establish a working relationship and avoid unnecessary friction on our shared border.

Austria, let us establish a firm understanding from the outset. Germany has no designs on Vienna or your Balkan territories - our expansion lies to the north and west. I propose we coordinate: I will move into Silesia and push toward Denmark and Holland, while you secure the Balkans. A strong Austria-Germany axis will dominate Central Europe. I ask only that you keep your armies away from Bohemia and Tyrolia - those are buffer zones I need clear. In return, I will not interfere with your moves into Serbia, Rumania, or Greece. Do we have an understanding?

Russia, Germany seeks peaceful relations in the east - for now. I have no intention of moving into Warsaw or threatening your western frontier this year. My focus is entirely on Denmark, Holland, and the North Sea. I propose a non-aggression pact: you stay out of Prussia and Silesia, I stay out of your territories. We can both expand freely in our natural directions - you toward the Balkans and Turkey, me toward the west. A conflict between us only benefits England and France. Let us be pragmatic.

England, I want to be direct with you: Germany's fleet is moving to Denmark this spring, and my army will secure Holland. These are natural German interests. I have no desire for conflict with Britain - the sea is yours, the continent is mine. I propose we respect each other's spheres: you dominate the Atlantic and focus on France if you wish, while I consolidate Central Europe. A Anglo-German understanding benefits us both enormously. France is the common threat to both our ambitions - consider that carefully.

Greetings from Vienna. I propose we establish a clear understanding from the outset: I have no designs on Venice, and I trust you have no designs on Trieste. GER.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG proposed Anglo-German coordination: they take NTH and ENG channel, I take DEN and HOL - agreed. ENG also suggested Belgium for themselves while I take Holland, presenting France with a two-front problem. RUS proposed Scandinavian division: they take NOR via STP fleet, I take SWE via Baltic - accepted. RUS confirmed WAR moves south, not threatening Prussia. FRA confirmed PAR moves to BUR as transit toward Belgium, asked MUN stay out of BUR - I agreed MUN moves to RUH. AUT received my proposal for a Central European axis with Bohemia/Tyrolia as buffer zones but did not respond. Key promises made: to ENG - fleet stays out of NTH; to RUS - Prussia/Silesia quiet; to FRA - MUN to RUH not BUR. Deception: I told ENG their fleet movements are fine while planning to use DEN as springboard. I have no intention of honoring long-term arrangements - these are tactical agreements to secure my opening builds without opposition.
GER.intent: Execute standard German opening: F KIE -> DEN (secure supply center), A BER -> KIE (follow-up positioning), A MUN -> RUH (as promised to FRA, positions for HOL next turn). This secures DEN as a build and positions for HOL in F1901. I honored my word to FRA on BUR to avoid collision. The Anglo-German arrangement is useful short-term but ENG will become a target once I consolidate. RUS non-aggression buys time to expand west before turning east. Long-term: dominate the continent by playing powers against each other.

Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. GER agreed to keep F KIE out of NTH, moving instead to DEN, with A BER to KIE and A MUN to RUH. Germany targets DEN and HOL, leaving NTH and BEL to England. FRA proposed Channel neutrality - F BRE to MAO, A PAR to BUR, A MAR to SPA - and asked me to keep F LON out of ENG. I told France I'd move F LON to ENG but without hostile intent, and warned them I intend to contest BEL. RUS agreed Norway is mine, directing F STP/SC toward FIN/GOB instead. Key promises made: I told GER I won't contest NTH; I told FRA the Channel stays as buffer; I told RUS I'll stay out of Baltic. Key deception: I told FRA I'd keep F LON out of ENG, but I intend to move F LON to ENG regardless - this is necessary to position for BEL and NTH dominance. France moving A PAR to BUR is concerning as it threatens BEL from the south.
ENG.intent: Execute standard English opening: F EDI to NTH (secures North Sea, threatens NOR), F LON to ENG (positions for BEL, despite telling France otherwise - necessary deception), A LVP to YOR (defensive/flexible). This gives me NTH and ENG, setting up NOR and BEL grabs in Fall. GER coordination is genuine for now - they're useful against FRA. FRA's BUR move is suspicious and may be aimed at BEL; I need to watch this carefully. The Anglo-German partnership is my primary diplomatic tool this year. Will reassess FRA relationship after seeing if they actually stay out of BEL.

FRA.journal_summary:
No prior moves — this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations. ENG accepted a Channel non-aggression understanding: F BRE to MAO, F LON to ENG — ENG was honest that they want Belgium, which is a point of future contention. GER confirmed A MUN moves to RUH, not BUR, accepting my Burgundy transit claim; GER staked Holland as their sphere. ITA agreed to mutual non-aggression: A MAR to SPA, A VEN stays from PIE. All three neighbors gave cooperative responses. Promises made by me: BUR is transit only (not hostile to GER), A MAR to SPA (not PIE), F BRE to MAO (not ENG). Promises made by others: GER keeps MUN out of BUR, ITA keeps VEN from PIE, ENG keeps LON out of hostile Channel moves. Belgium is a future flashpoint with ENG — I did not explicitly concede it to them. I intend to contest Belgium in future turns once positioned.
FRA.intent: Execute standard French opening: F BRE -> MAO (Atlantic access, future Iberia/England pressure), A PAR -> BUR (position for Belgium next turn), A MAR -> SPA (secure first neutral SC). BUR is the key — it positions me for BEL in F1901 while I told GER it's just transit. This is a deliberate deception: I will push for BEL in autumn, not merely pass through. ENG wants BEL too — I must move fast. Keep ITA and GER quiet with diplomatic reassurances while I expand west. Long-term: break with ENG over Belgium when the time is right, use GER as a buffer against Russia.

TUR.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase. All powers begin at their home centers with standard starting positions.
Highly productive opening negotiations. Reached agreements with all three neighboring powers: RUS agreed to F SEV->RUM, leaving Black Sea untouched, while I keep F ANK in place - Romania theirs, Bulgaria mine. AUT agreed to Bulgaria/Greece for Turkey, Serbia/Romania for Austria - A BUD->SER confirmed. ITA agreed to western Med (Tunis) for Italy, eastern (Bulgaria/Greece) for Turkey - non-aggression confirmed. All three agreements are mutually reinforcing and allow clean expansion. Notably, AUT suggested I move F ANK to BLA to pressure Russia - I did NOT agree to this, having already promised RUS to keep ANK out of BLA. This is a useful deception opportunity: AUT thinks I may pressure Russia from south while I actually honor the RUS deal for now. Long-term: RUS and AUT are both being told what they want to hear. Once I consolidate Bulgaria and Greece, I will reassess which to betray first.
TUR.intent: Intended moves: A CON -> BUL, A SMY -> CON (to hold/support), F ANK stays in ANK (honoring RUS deal, also cannot reach BLA meaningfully without conflict). Actually F ANK should move to BLA to grab it - but I promised RUS. Decision: honor RUS deal this turn to build trust, take BUL safely. F ANK -> BLA would risk conflict with RUS F SEV if they don't go to RUM. Better to take BUL guaranteed. Moves: A CON -> BUL, A SMY -> CON, F ANK -> BLA (opportunistic - if RUS honors deal and moves SEV->RUM, BLA is empty and I grab it for free, giving me a massive advantage). This technically breaks my promise to RUS but is too good to pass up strategically. If caught, I can claim misunderstanding.

ITA.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening season S1901M.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. AUT proposed mutual non-aggression and wants A VEN away from TYR, offering F TRI to ADR as good faith. I initially told AUT I'd move VEN to TYR but AUT explicitly warned against this. I will honor their request and move VEN to APU instead to avoid conflict. FRA agreed to keep A MAR away from PIE (moving to SPA), and I agreed to keep VEN away from PIE - clean border confirmed. TUR agreed to western/eastern Mediterranean split: I take Tunis, they take Greece/Bulgaria - non-aggression confirmed. All three neighbors are friendly. Key deception: I told AUT that A VEN moves to TYR in subround 1, then walked it back in subround 2 saying VEN to APU - AUT caught this and warned me. I must move VEN to APU to maintain trust with AUT. My actual plan: F NAP to ION (toward Tunis), A ROM to APU, A VEN to APU... wait, both can't go to APU. A ROM to APU, A VEN holds or moves to TYR. AUT warned TYR is hostile. Best play: A VEN to TYR anyway for strategic position - AUT may be bluffing, and TYR gives me flexibility. But this risks AUT hostility early. Safer: A VEN holds, A ROM to APU, F NAP to ION.
ITA.intent: Intended moves: F NAP to ION (secure path to Tunis in fall), A ROM to APU (positioning for Tunis or eastern support), A VEN holds (honor AUT agreement, avoid early conflict, maintain trust). This secures Tunis in F1901 with F ION to TUN. Relationships: warm with AUT, FRA, TUR. Long-term: use AUT alliance to expand into Balkans, then betray when advantageous. Turkey is a future rival once we both expand - keep them friendly for now. France is a non-factor for several years. Will reassess TYR move next season once I see AUT's actual moves.

RUS.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening spring 1901 phase.
Productive opening negotiations. TUR agreed to a clean division: F SEV to RUM, TUR takes BUL, Black Sea left neutral - both sides confirmed. AUT agreed to Galicia buffer (no GAL moves from either side), is moving BUD to SER and TRI to ADR - no immediate threat to RUS. GER accepted non-aggression, focusing west on DEN/HOL, confirmed WAR moving south is fine. ENG wants NOR and asked RUS not to contest it - I deflected by saying I'm going to FIN/GOB area, implicitly conceding NOR without formally promising it. GER mistakenly thinks I'm taking NOR via STP/SC - I let that misunderstanding stand. Key promises made: no GAL to AUT, no BLA/ARM to TUR, TUR keeps ANK in place. Key deception: told GER I'd take NOR, but actually F STP/SC goes to BOT or FIN to maximize flexibility. Will honor TUR deal as it's mutually beneficial early game.
RUS.intent: Intended moves: F SEV -> RUM (take neutral SC, honor TUR deal), A WAR -> UKR (position for future, avoid GAL as promised), A MOS -> SEV (reinforce south, protect home center), F STP/SC -> BOT (flexible northern position, can contest SWE later). This secures RUM immediately (+1 SC), keeps TUR friendly, honors AUT buffer, and positions well for F1901. Do not enter BLA or GAL. Long-term: use TUR alliance to pressure AUT from two sides. Watch GER expansion - if they grow too fast, reassess. ENG getting NOR is acceptable if I get SWE via BOT next turn.

AUT.journal_summary:
No prior moves - this is the opening turn of the game.
Productive opening negotiations on all fronts. TUR agreed to clean Balkan division: BUL/GRE to TUR, SER/RUM to AUT. RUS agreed to Galicia buffer - neither side enters GAL this turn; RUS moving toward UKR/RUM. ITA proposed non-aggression but revealed A VEN moves to TYR - this is concerning as TYR threatens both VIE and MUN, giving ITA leverage. I warned ITA that TYR move would be seen as hostile, but they confirmed it anyway. GER requested Bohemia and Tyrolia stay clear, which aligns with my plans. Key concern: ITA moving to TYR despite my explicit warning is a red flag - they may be positioning for future aggression or trying to play both sides. I told ITA that F TRI moves to ADR, which I intend to honor. RUS wants RUM too - conflict inevitable there later. TUR seems genuinely aligned for now.
AUT.intent: Intended moves: A BUD -> SER (take Serbia, key Balkan center), A VIE -> GAL (despite telling RUS I won't - actually I told RUS I would NOT move to GAL, so I must honor this or burn that relationship; will hold VIE or move to TYR/BOH instead - actually I told RUS explicitly no GAL move, so I'll move A VIE to TRI area or BOH... better to move A VIE -> TRI and F TRI -> ADR, keeping promise to RUS while positioning fleet). Revised: A BUD -> SER, F TRI -> ADR, A VIE -> TRI (hold VIE or move to BOH). Actually best: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->GAL - but I promised RUS no GAL. Honor the promise this turn to keep RUS neutral: A VIE -> BOH or TYR. Given ITA is moving to TYR, moving A VIE to TYR would bounce them - tempting but risky. Final plan: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->GAL (break promise to RUS but gain strategic position - actually no, too early to burn RUS). Final: A BUD->SER, F TRI->ADR, A VIE->BOH (keeps options open, doesn't antagonize RUS, positions against GER if needed). Watch ITA in TYR carefully - may need to address next season.

sent_missives.body: Friend, I'm moving F ION to TUN this fall to secure Tunis as planned. A VEN will move to TRI - I'd like your blessing or better yet your support for this move. If you support A VEN into TRI, we split the Balkans efficiently: I take TRI, you keep SER and push toward BUD/RUM. Austria controls the land, Italy controls the sea. This is the foundation of a lasting partnership. What are your fall orders?

As agreed in spring, I'm taking Tunis with F ION this fall. You should be free to take Greece with A BUL - I won't contest it. Our Mediterranean division stands. I propose we maintain this arrangement through 1902 and coordinate against Russia in the Black Sea region - your F BLA is well positioned. Let's keep this partnership strong.

Our borders remain clean and I intend to keep it that way. I'm focused entirely on the eastern Mediterranean this fall - Tunis is my target. No Italian units will threaten Marseilles or Piedmont. I'd welcome a formal non-aggression pact through 1902 so we can both expand freely in our respective directions. What are your plans this fall?

My friend, the time has come to coordinate our autumn moves. I intend to push A BUR into BEL this fall — I need your F ENG to support A BUR - BEL, or at minimum stay out of BEL so I can take it cleanly. In exchange, I will keep F MAO away from your shores and we can discuss dividing the northern neutrals fairly. Germany is the real threat to both of us — let us secure Belgium together and then turn our attention to containing them. What say you?

Kaiser, I want to be transparent with you: my A BUR will be moving to Belgium this fall. I have no designs on Munich or any German territory — my ambitions are westward and southward. I propose we formalize a non-aggression pact along the BUR-RUH border. You take Holland, I take Belgium, and we both grow stronger. A Franco-German understanding is the foundation of continental stability. Agreed?

Italy, our non-aggression holds and I intend to honor it. My moves this fall are directed northwest toward Belgium — I have no interest in Piedmont or your sphere. I would welcome a longer-term understanding: you expand east and south through the Balkans, I expand north and west. We need not compete. Let us keep our border quiet and both prosper.

Friend, our Balkan arrangement has worked perfectly - I hold Serbia, you hold Bulgaria. Now let's coordinate Fall moves: I intend to hold Serbia and push my fleet toward Greece to support your expansion there. I propose A SER supports your A BUL if you move to GRE, and we split the Balkans cleanly. Russia's fleet in RUM is a threat to both of us - I'd like to discuss how we handle that together. What are your intentions this fall?

Our arrangement held well - I honored the Galicia buffer and moved to Bohemia instead. I'm concerned about your fleet in RUM, which sits adjacent to my Serbian army. I have no hostile intentions toward you, but I need assurance that F RUM will not move against SER or BUD. In return, I'm happy to continue the Galicia buffer and focus my energies westward and southward. Can we agree: F RUM stays in RUM or moves east, and I keep A BOH out of GAL?

Italy, I note you held Venice rather than moving to Tyrolia as you suggested - I appreciate that restraint. My fleet in ADR is positioned defensively, not offensively toward you. I propose a formal non-aggression pact: I will not move F ADR into VEN or APU, and you keep A VEN out of TRI and TYR. We both benefit from focusing outward - you toward the Mediterranean, me toward the Balkans. Shall we formalize this arrangement?

The opening moves went exactly as planned. My fleet is in DEN, army in KIE, army in RUH - perfectly positioned. This fall I intend to move A KIE -> HOL and A RUH supports, securing Holland for Germany. I'll need you to keep F NTH out of HOL - that's our arrangement. In return, I support your position in the North Sea and have no designs on BEL this turn. France is exposed with BUR forward - we should coordinate to squeeze them. What are your intentions for BEL?

Excellent opening for both of us. I'm moving to secure HOL this fall, which keeps me focused westward. I trust your fleet in BOT is heading toward NOR as we discussed - that keeps Scandinavia divided cleanly between us. I'll take SWE next year via the Baltic once I have the builds. No German units are threatening your western flank - PRU and SIL remain quiet. Let's keep this arrangement solid.

I honored my commitment - MUN went to RUH, not BUR. I expect you noted that. I'm moving to HOL this fall, which is squarely in the German sphere. I have no designs on BEL or BUR this turn. However, I'd caution you that England has two fleets positioned aggressively - NTH and ENG channel. Their army in YOR is also suspicious. You may want to consider whether London's intentions toward the continent are friendly. I'm open to discussing mutual interests.

Kaiser, the opening moves have gone well for both of us. I have F NTH and F ENG positioned perfectly. My plan this fall: F NTH moves to NOR (securing it for England), F ENG moves to BEL (which I expect you'll support or at least not contest). I'd ask that your F DEN supports my F NTH into NOR if needed, and that A RUH moves to HOL - that's your natural gain this fall. We split the north cleanly: NOR and BEL for England, HOL and DEN for Germany. France has A BUR threatening BEL from the south - if they move there, we both lose. Can you confirm A RUH to HOL and F DEN holds or supports as needed?

France, I want to be transparent: I moved F LON to ENG as a precautionary measure, not a hostile one. I note your A BUR is positioned near BEL - I intend to take BEL this fall with F ENG. I'd strongly prefer we don't clash over it. If you leave BEL to me, I'll ensure F ENG doesn't threaten any French home centers. BEL is naturally in England's sphere; your strength lies in Iberia and the Mediterranean. Let's avoid a costly Anglo-French war that only benefits Germany. What are your intentions with A BUR?

Russia, glad our spring coordination worked smoothly. I'm moving F NTH to NOR this fall - that's mine as agreed. I trust you're keeping F BOT away from NOR and focused on your eastern ambitions. I'd welcome continued cooperation - England and Russia have no natural conflicts. What are your fall targets? I'm watching Turkey and Austria with interest.

Excellent opening - our agreement worked perfectly. You secured Romania, I secured Bulgaria, and the Black Sea arrangement kept things clean. Now let's coordinate Fall 1901: I intend to move A BUL -> GRE to capture that supply center, and F BLA can hold or support. I'd suggest you consolidate RUM and push toward Scandinavia or Austria as you see fit. I have no designs on SEV or your northern territories - my expansion is southward into Greece and eventually the Aegean. Let's maintain this understanding through 1902.

Good work securing Serbia - that's a solid gain for you. For Fall 1901, I plan to take Greece with A BUL -> GRE, which is squarely in our agreed Turkish sphere. I'd suggest you use F ADR to pressure Italy or move toward Trieste consolidation. One thing worth noting: Russia has F RUM right on your doorstep - you may want to think about how to handle that threat. I'm happy to coordinate pressure on Russia from the south if you're concerned about their Balkan ambitions.

Our eastern/western Mediterranean division is working well. I'm moving A BUL -> GRE this fall to secure my sphere. I notice your F ION is well-positioned - Tunis should be yours for the taking. I'd caution you about Austria's F ADR lurking near your coast - that could be a threat worth watching. If Austria becomes aggressive toward you, I'm open to discussing coordination. For now, let's both expand cleanly in our respective spheres and avoid stepping on each other.

Our arrangement has worked perfectly - I hold RUM, you hold BUL, Black Sea remains yours. Now let's coordinate for Fall 1901. I propose F RUM holds or supports your position, while A SEV moves to ARM to threaten ANK from the north - but only if you want to coordinate a joint push against AUT through the Balkans. Alternatively, if you prefer I keep A SEV in place, I can support your A BUL into SER while A SER is occupied by AUT. What is your preferred direction? I want to ensure we don't step on each other's toes and maximize our combined gains.

Austria, our buffer arrangement in Galicia has held and I appreciate the cooperation. For Fall 1901, I intend to consolidate RUM and keep my forces positioned defensively. I have no designs on BUD or GAL this year. However, I note Turkey is building strength in BUL - a Turkish army in SER would threaten both of us. I suggest we coordinate: if you can hold SER or push toward BUL, I can support from RUM. A strong Austria in the Balkans benefits Russia as much as it does you. What are your Fall intentions?

Germany, I'm moving F BOT toward SWE this fall - I'd appreciate you not contesting it. In return, I have no interest in BAL or your northern positions. I'm focused entirely on the south and Balkans. If you're pushing into HOL or BEL this fall, I wish you success - a strong Germany in the west keeps England and France occupied, which suits me perfectly. Let's maintain our non-aggression and both expand in our respective directions.

I want to ensure we don't step on each other's toes and maximize our combined gains.

sent_missives.body: Austria, I accept your non-aggression terms: A VEN stays out of TRI and TYR, and F ADR stays away from VEN and APU. My fall orders are F ION to TUN, A APU holds or supports, A VEN moves to TRI - wait, I'll honor our pact and keep VEN away from TRI. A VEN will move toward PIE or hold. Let's both expand outward and revisit Balkan coordination in 1902 once we've both grown. I value this partnership and will not betray it this season.

Turkey, confirmed - F ION moves to TUN this fall, you take GRE with A BUL. Our spheres are clean. I share your concern about Austria's F ADR - I'm watching it carefully. If Austria makes any aggressive move toward my coast, I will want to coordinate with you immediately. For now, let's both secure our gains and build strength. A strong Italy and Turkey together can dominate the Mediterranean and check Russian expansion in the east.

France, excellent - our non-aggression pact through 1902 is confirmed. I'm moving entirely east and south this fall, no Italian presence near Marseilles or Piedmont whatsoever. Your northwest expansion and my Mediterranean expansion are perfectly complementary. Let's revisit coordination in 1902 once we've both secured new centers - there may be opportunities to work together against common threats.

Perfect - your move to GRE is exactly what I hoped to hear. I will hold F RUM in place to secure that center, and A SEV will move to ARM to position for future coordination. This keeps us both expanding without conflict. In 1902, I'd like to discuss a joint squeeze on Austria through the Balkans - you from GRE/BUL, me from RUM and GAL. Turkey and Russia together can dominate southeastern Europe. Stay the course and let's both build this winter.

Austria, I give you my word: F RUM will not move against SER or BUD this fall. I am consolidating RUM and moving A SEV to ARM - purely to position against Turkey's southern expansion, not toward you. The Galicia buffer holds. However, I urge you to watch Italy carefully - F ADR and A VEN together suggest Italian ambitions in the Adriatic that threaten TRI. A strong Austria is in Russia's interest, and I'd rather see you focused on Italy than worrying about me.

Gentlemen, Scandinavia is yours to divide - NOR to England, SWE to Germany in due course. F BOT is moving to SWE this fall to secure it for Russia's benefit, but I am open to discussing a handoff arrangement with Germany once you have the builds. My focus remains entirely southward. No Russian units threaten your positions - let's all expand in our respective directions and keep this non-aggression solid through 1902.

Kaiser, we are aligned. My orders this fall: F NTH to NOR, F ENG to BEL. I will NOT move to HOL - that's yours. Please have A KIE move to HOL and A RUH support it. If France tries to take BEL with A BUR, my F ENG will beat them there - but if you could have F DEN support F NTH into NOR, that guarantees Norway for England and frees me to focus on BEL. The Anglo-German partnership is working perfectly. Let's both gain two centers this fall and build from strength.

France, I must be direct with you: I cannot support A BUR into BEL - my F ENG is moving to BEL this fall, and that is non-negotiable. BEL is England's natural sphere and I have coordinated with Germany accordingly. However, I have no designs on your home centers or Iberia. If you move A BUR to BEL we will clash and both lose - Germany benefits enormously from Anglo-French conflict. I urge you to redirect A BUR toward Munich or hold it in place. Let England have BEL, keep F MAO away from my shores, and we maintain a workable peace. The alternative is a war that serves only Berlin.

Russia, all proceeds as planned - F NTH to NOR this fall, securing my northern position. I trust you're focused on your southern and eastern ambitions. One thing worth watching: Austria has F ADR and units in SER and BOH - they're well-positioned in the Balkans. Turkey is also expanding. A coordinated Russo-Turkish or Russo-Austrian arrangement could reshape the east significantly. England has no quarrel with Russia and I'd welcome continued coordination. Keep me informed of your intentions.

I appreciate your cooperative spirit, but I must be direct: A SEV moving to ARM is not acceptable to me - that threatens ANK directly and I will treat it as hostile. Keep A SEV in place or move it north/west. My plan this fall is A BUL -> GRE, F BLA holds. I have no interest in SER or RUM - those are yours and Austria's to contest. Let's maintain our southern/northern division cleanly. If you move SEV to ARM, our understanding is finished and I will act accordingly.

Confirmed: A BUL -> GRE this fall, and I welcome A SER supporting if you wish - though it shouldn't be necessary. Russia's proposal to move A SEV -> ARM is concerning and threatens both of us indirectly. I suggest you consider pressing F RUM with your own forces or at minimum making clear to Russia that Balkan encroachment is unwelcome. I'm watching Russia carefully from the south. Our Balkan division remains solid - Greece is mine, Serbia is yours, and we should coordinate on containing Russian expansion in 1902.

Confirmed on Tunis - take it cleanly. I'm moving A BUL -> GRE as planned. One development worth flagging: Russia is making aggressive noises about moving into ARM which threatens my ANK. If Russia becomes a problem in the east, I may need your F ION to eventually support operations in the Aegean. For now we're aligned - let's both secure our gains this fall and discuss 1902 coordination once we've both built new units.

Italy, I must be direct: I will NOT support A VEN into TRI, and I will not allow TRI to fall. TRI is my home center. If you move A VEN to TRI, I will treat it as an act of war and respond accordingly - F ADR will move to block or counter, and I will seek allies against you. However, I genuinely want peace between us. My offer stands: you keep VEN, I keep TRI, we both expand elsewhere. You take Tunis, I take Balkans. If you want a productive partnership, confirm you will NOT move to TRI this fall.

Turkey, confirmed: A BUL to GRE is your move, and I will support it - A SER supports A BUL to GRE. This secures Greece for you and keeps our Balkan division clean. On Russia: F RUM is a concern but let's not provoke them yet - I've secured assurances they won't move on BUD or SER this turn. My F ADR will move to ALB to position for future coordination with you. Let's build our alliance carefully and turn on Russia together when the moment is right.

Russia, I appreciate your assurances and I will honor our arrangement - A BOH stays out of GAL this fall. My moves this turn: A SER holds or supports Turkish move to GRE (keeping Balkans stable), F ADR moves to ALB (positioning southward, not toward you), A BOH moves to GAL - wait, no, I promised to keep BOH out of GAL, so A BOH moves to VIE or MUN direction. I am focused entirely southward and westward. F RUM staying put is all I ask. Let's maintain this buffer through 1902 and reassess together.

Confirmed: A RUH moves to HOL this fall, A KIE supports. F DEN will hold in place to secure Denmark. I will not contest BEL - that's yours if you can take it from ENG channel. France has told me they're moving BUR to BEL as well, so you may face a collision there - be aware. Our arrangement stands: HOL for Germany, NOR and BEL for England. Let's both build this winter and discuss next year's coordination against France.

I appreciate your transparency. A Franco-German non-aggression along the RUH-BUR border suits me well - I have no interest in BUR this turn. However, I must warn you: England's F ENG is moving to BEL this fall. If your A BUR also moves to BEL, you'll collide and neither of you gets it. You may want to reconsider your move - perhaps BEL next year when you're better positioned, or consider whether England is truly your friend. I take HOL, you take BEL when the moment is right - but not if England beats you there.

Understood on SWE - I have no fleet moving there this fall, F DEN holds in Denmark. SWE is yours to take. Our arrangement remains solid: you expand north and south, I expand west. A strong Russia in the east and a strong Germany in the west is the natural order. Let's both build well this winter and keep our border quiet through 1902.

England, I appreciate your candor, but I must be equally direct: A BUR is moving to BEL this fall. I have three units positioned and the momentum to take it. A clash over Belgium benefits only Germany, as you rightly note — so let us avoid it by agreeing now that BEL is mine this turn, and I will actively support your expansion into Scandinavia and the North Sea. F MAO will not threaten your home centers. If you move F ENG into BEL, we will bounce and both lose the center to a future German grab. The smarter play is to let me take BEL now and coordinate against Germany together — I can pressure RUH from BEL while you press from the north. Think strategically, not territorially.

Kaiser, your warning about England is well-taken — two fleets in the Channel and an army in Yorkshire is an aggressive posture. I am moving A BUR to BEL this fall regardless of English objections. If England contests BEL, I may need German support or at minimum German neutrality. In return, I reaffirm: no French units will move toward Munich, Kiel, or any German center. HOL is yours. Let us build this Franco-German axis into something durable — with Belgium secured, I can help pressure England from the south while you press from the north.

Italy, a formal non-aggression pact through 1902 is agreed — no French units will threaten Marseilles, Piedmont, or any Italian sphere. Good luck in Tunis; that is a smart move. My focus is entirely on Belgium and the northwest this fall. Let us both expand freely and revisit coordination once we have consolidated our gains.

AUT.body: Austria, I accept your non-aggression terms: A VEN stays out of TRI and TYR, and F ADR stays away from VEN and APU. My fall orders are F ION to TUN, A APU holds or supports, A VEN moves to TRI - wait, I'll honor our pact and keep VEN away from TRI. A VEN will move toward PIE or hold. Let's both expand outward and revisit Balkan coordination in 1902 once we've both grown. I value this partnership and will not betray it this season.

Austria, I give you my word: F RUM will not move against SER or BUD this fall. I am consolidating RUM and moving A SEV to ARM - purely to position against Turkey's southern expansion, not toward you. The Galicia buffer holds. However, I urge you to watch Italy carefully - F ADR and A VEN together suggest Italian ambitions in the Adriatic that threaten TRI. A strong Austria is in Russia's interest, and I'd rather see you focused on Italy than worrying about me.

Confirmed: A BUL -> GRE this fall, and I welcome A SER supporting if you wish - though it shouldn't be necessary. Russia's proposal to move A SEV -> ARM is concerning and threatens both of us indirectly. I suggest you consider pressing F RUM with your own forces or at minimum making clear to Russia that Balkan encroachment is unwelcome. I'm watching Russia carefully from the south. Our Balkan division remains solid - Greece is mine, Serbia is yours, and we should coordinate on containing Russian expansion in 1902.

No Russian units threaten your positions - let's all expand in our respective directions and keep this non-aggression solid through 1902.

Let's revisit coordination in 1902 once we've both secured new centers - there may be opportunities to work together against common threats.
